It has been shown that $\# P(t)$ satisfies a Margulis-type asymptotic estimate, meaning there is an elementary closed-form function $f(h, t)$ such that $\lim_{t\to \infty} \frac{\#P(t)}{f(h, t)} = 1$. What is the expression for $f(h, t)$?

Let $a,b,c \in \mathbb{Z}\setminus\{0\}$ and let $m,n \ge 2$ be integers. It is proven that the equation $ax+by = cw^mz^n$ is partition regular over $\mathbb{Z}\setminus\{0\}$ if and only if $b = k a$ for a specific integer constant $k$. What is the value of $k$?

Let $n \ge 11$ be an integer and let $f \in C^{0,1}_{\text{loc}}(\mathbb R)$ with $f \ge 0$. A sharp Liouville property states that any stable solution $u \in C^2(\mathbb R^n)$ to the semilinear elliptic equation $-\Delta u = f(u)$ in \mathbb R^n satisfying the growth condition $|u(x)| = o(|x|^{\alpha_n})$ as $|x| \to +\infty$ must be a constant. What is the optimal exponent $\alpha_n$ as a function of $n$?

Consider the higher-rank generalised Racah algebra $R(n)$ associated with $n$-dimensional superintegrable systems with coalgebra symmetry. The left and right partial Casimir invariants of the coalgebra generate a certain number of quadratic subalgebras. At a fixed integer $n > 3$, these quadratic subalgebra structures can be understood as the images of $k$ injective morphisms of the rank-one Racah algebra $R(3)$ into $R(n)$. What is the value of $k$ as a function of $n$?

Let $(M, g)$ be an $n$-dimensional perfect fluid spacetime (with $n > 1$) having pressure $p$ and energy density $\sigma$. Suppose that the velocity vector field of the spacetime is a concircular vector field and the spacetime admits a second-order symmetric parallel tensor. If this parallel tensor is not a constant multiple of the metric tensor $g$, the equation of state of the perfect fluid is determined to be of the form $p = c \sigma$. What is the coefficient $c$ as a function of the dimension $n$?

A characterization of the Wishart distribution states that if $W$ is an $n \times n$ positive-definite symmetric matrix of random variables with a probability density function, then $W$ follows a Wishart distribution if and only if the Schur complement $W_{11} - W_{12} W_{22}^{-1} W_{12}'$ is independent of $\{W_{12},W_{22}\}$ for every block partitioning $W = \begin{pmatrix} W_{11} & W_{12} \\ W_{12}' & W_{22} \end{pmatrix}$. What is the minimum integer value of $n$ required for this characterization to hold?

Consider a kernel-based two-sample test related to the Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) where data densities $p$ and $q$ are supported on a compact $d$-dimensional Riemannian sub-manifold $\mathcal{M}$ without boundary, isometrically embedded in an $m$-dimensional Euclidean space, and are Hölder continuous of order $\beta$ (where $0 < \beta \le 2$) on $\mathcal{M}$. Let $\Delta_2$ denote the squared $L^2$-divergence between $p$ and $q$ on the manifold. It is proven that for a large number of samples $n$ and optimally chosen kernel bandwidth scaling as $n^{-1/(d+4\beta)}$, small density departures can be detected provided that $\Delta_2$ is greater than a constant multiple of $n^{-x}$. What is the exact value of the exponent $x$ as a function of $d$ and $\beta$?

Consider the binary symplectic polar space $W(7,2)$ whose points canonically encode $4$-qubit observables. What is the total number of negative lines possessed by $W(7,2)$ (where a line is called negative if the ordinary product of the observables located on it is $-\mathcal{I}_4$)?

Consider the frequency-dependent single-channel reflection of waves from a cavity with spatially uniform losses that supports chaotic ray dynamics. The non-orthogonality factors $O_{nn}$ of the eigenmodes associated with the non-selfadjoint effective Hamiltonian are heavy-tail distributed. Using random matrix theory, the explicit probability density $\mathcal{P}(O_{nn})$ for systems with both broken and preserved time reversal symmetry exhibits a universal tail $\mathcal{P}(O_{nn} \gg 1) \sim O_{nn}^{\alpha}$. What is the exact numerical value of the exponent $\alpha$?
What is the value of the extremal length systole of the Bolza surface?

Let $T$ be a tree of order $n \geq 2$. The arithmetic-geometric matrix $A_{ag}(T)$ of $T$ is an $n \times n$ matrix where the $(i,j)$-entry is $\frac{d_i+d_j}{2\sqrt{d_i d_j}}$ if vertices $v_i$ and $v_j$ are adjacent (with $d_i$ being the degree of vertex $v_i$), and $0$ otherwise. Let $\rho_{ag}(T)$ be the spectral radius of $A_{ag}(T)$. As a closed-form expression in terms of $n$, what is the exact maximum value of $\rho_{ag}(T)$ over all trees of order $n$?

Evaluate the exact value of the infinite series \( \sum_{k=1}^{\infty}\frac{H_{k-1}4^k}{k^2\binom{2k}{k}} \), where \( H_n = \sum_{j=1}^n \frac{1}{j} \) denotes the \(n\)-th harmonic number (with \( H_0=0 \)).

Consider random surfaces defined on finite volumes of $\mathbb{Z}^d$ with a uniformly convex gradient interaction in the presence of quenched disorder given by an independent, symmetric, random external field with finite second moment. With Dirichlet boundary conditions, as the thermodynamic limit is taken, what is the minimum integer dimension $d$ for which the distribution of the surface itself is established to converge to a translation-covariant Gibbs measure?

In a graph $G$, a set of edges $S$ is called an edge general position set if no three edges from $S$ lie on a common shortest path (geodesic) in $G$. The edge general position number, denoted ${\rm gp_e}(G)$, is the maximum possible size of an edge general position set in $G$. Let $Q_r$ denote the $r$-dimensional hypercube graph. What is the exact value of ${\rm gp_e}(Q_r)$ as a function of $r$, for $r \geq 2$?

A rank-$r$ integer matrix $A$ is $\Delta$-modular if the determinant of each $r \times r$ submatrix has absolute value at most $\Delta$. For each sufficiently large integer $r$, what is the maximum number of nonzero, pairwise non-parallel columns of a rank-$r$ $2$-modular matrix?

In combinatorics on words, let $\Delta$ be a set of variables and $\Delta^R = \{V^R \mid V \in \Delta\}$ be their reversals. A pattern $p$ with reversal is a word over $\Delta \cup \Delta^R$. A word $w$ over an alphabet $\Sigma$ is said to avoid $p$ if there is no factor $f$ of $w$ such that $f=h(p)$, where $h: (\Delta \cup \Delta^R)^* \to \Sigma^*$ is a non-erasing morphism such that $h(V^R)$ is the mirror image (reversal) of the word $h(V)$ for every $V \in \Delta$. A pattern $p$ is $k$-avoidable if there exists an infinite word over a $k$-letter alphabet that avoids $p$. A pattern $p$ is called *doubled* if every variable $V \in \Delta$ that appears in $p$ appears at least twice in total (counting occurrences of both $V$ and $V^R$). What is the smallest positive integer $k$ such that every doubled pattern with reversal is $k$-avoidable?
Up to projective equivalence over $\mathbb{F}_4$, what is the exact number of maximal plane curves of degree $3$ over $\mathbb{F}_4$?

Let $G$ be a simple graph with vertex set $V(G)$, edge set $E(G)$, maximum degree $\Delta(G)$, minimum degree $\delta(G)$, and chromatic index $\chi'(G)$. A graph $G$ is said to be $\Delta$-critical if $\chi'(G)=\Delta(G)+1$ and $\chi'(H)\le \Delta(G)$ for every proper subgraph $H$ of $G$. $G$ is overfull if $|E(G)|>\Delta(G)\lfloor |V(G)|/2 \rfloor$. A theorem establishes that any $\Delta$-critical graph $G$ is overfull if $4\Delta(G) - a\delta(G) \ge 3|V(G)| - b$, where $a$ and $b$ are specific coprime positive integers. What is the ordered pair $(a, b)$?

Let $q$ be a prime power and $k$ be an integer such that $2 \leq k < q$. Consider families $\mathcal{F}$ of polynomials over the finite field $\mathbb{F}_q$ of degree at most $k$ with the property that any two polynomials $f, g \in \mathcal{F}$ take the same value on at least one point (i.e., there exists $x \in \mathbb{F}_q$ such that $f(x) = g(x)$). What is the maximum possible number of polynomials in such a family $\mathcal{F}$?

Let $k$ be a field of positive characteristic $p$. Let $G$ be the group of points of a semisimple connected smooth algebraic group defined over a local field of residue characteristic $p$. Let $Z(G)$ denote the center of $G$. Let $\mathfrak{Z}(G)$ denote the Bernstein center of $G$, which is defined as the $k$-algebra of natural endomorphisms of the identity functor on the category of smooth $k$-linear representations of $G$. In terms of $k$ and $Z(G)$, what is the $k$-algebra $\mathfrak{Z}(G)$ explicitly equal to?

Let $g \geq 2$ be an integer base and $b$ be a positive integer such that at least one prime factor of $b$ does not divide $g$. Let $(a_k)_{k=1}^\infty$ be a sequence of positive integers relatively prime to $b$. For any fixed integer $j \ge 1$ and any sequence of $j$ digits $D \in \{0, 1, \dots, g-1\}^j$, let $f_k(D)$ denote the relative frequency of the sequence $D$ occurring in the $g$-ary repetend (the repeating part of the fractional expansion) of $a_k/b^k$. What is $\lim_{k \to \infty} f_k(D)$?

Let $T_G(x,y)$ be the Tutte polynomial of a graph $G$, and let $v(G)$ denote its number of vertices. Consider a sequence $(G_n)_{n=1}^\infty$ of $4$-regular graphs such that their girth $g(G_n) \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$. What is the exact value of the limit $\lim_{n\to \infty} \left( T_{G_n}(2,1) \right)^{1/v(G_n)}$?

Consider the semi-random graph process starting with an empty graph on $n$ vertices. In each round, a vertex $u$ is presented to the player independently and uniformly at random. The player then adaptively selects a vertex $v$ and adds the undirected edge $uv$ to the graph. Let $G$ be an arbitrary fixed graph with degeneracy $d \ge 2$, and let $\omega(n)$ be any function tending to infinity as $n \to \infty$. It is known that the player can construct a subgraph isomorphic to $G$ asymptotically almost surely in at most $n^{\alpha} \omega(n)$ rounds. It was conjectured, and recently proven, that this bound is sharp for all graphs $G$, meaning that asymptotically almost surely it takes at least $n^{\alpha} / \omega(n)$ rounds to construct a subgraph isomorphic to $G$ under any strategy. What is the exact value of the exponent $\alpha$ as a function of the degeneracy $d$?

Let $M$ be a closed $n$-manifold with positive scalar curvature and abelian fundamental group. It has been proven that $M$ admits a finite covering $M'$ whose classifying map $u: M' 	o K(\pi_1(M'), 1)$ can be deformed to the $d$-skeleton (a property referred to as $M'$ being strongly inessential). This theorem holds for all $n$-manifolds with the exception of $4$-manifolds with spin universal coverings. What is the dimension $d$ of the skeleton, expressed as a function of $n$?

Let $s$ and $t$ be natural numbers, and let $\sigma$ be an $s$-core partition and $	au$ be a $t$-core partition. Let $d = \gcd(s,t)$ and $m = \operatorname{lcm}(s,t)$. Assume $\operatorname{core}_d(\sigma) = \operatorname{core}_d(\tau)$. Let $N_{\sigma, \tau}(k)$ be the number of $m$-core partitions of length no greater than $k$ whose $s$-core is $\sigma$ and whose $t$-core is $\tau$. For sufficiently large $k$, $N_{\sigma, \tau}(k)$ is a quasipolynomial in $k$ of period $m$. What is the degree of this quasipolynomial in terms of $s$, $t$, and $d$?

Let $\mathfrak{gl}_n$ be the general linear Lie algebra over an algebraically closed field of characteristic not equal to $2$ or $3$. For integers $n \geq 4$ and $r \geq 7$, what is the dimension of the variety of commuting $r$-tuples of nilpotent elements of $\mathfrak{gl}_n$?

The line graph $\Gamma$ of a multi-graph $\Delta$ is defined as the graph whose vertices are the edges of $\Delta$, where two such edges are adjacent in $\Gamma$ if and only if they meet in exactly one vertex of $\Delta$. It has been shown that a connected ordinary graph is the line graph of a multi-graph if and only if it does not contain any of a finite set of $N$ forbidden induced subgraphs. These forbidden induced subgraphs correspond to bases of anisotropic vectors of a $6$-dimensional orthogonal geometry of $-$-type over the field with two elements, or equivalently, to sets of $6$ generating reflections in the Weyl group of type $E_6$. What is the number $N$ of these forbidden induced subgraphs?

In the analysis of escaping saddle points using stochastic gradient descent (SGD) augmented with gradient compression, consider the setting where the objective function is $d$-dimensional with Lipschitz gradient and Lipschitz Hessian, and is accessed via an unbiased stochastic gradient oracle with bounded variance that is not assumed to be Lipschitz. When using the RandomK compressor to find an $\varepsilon$-second-order stationary point ($\varepsilon$-SOSP), the algorithm requires the same number of iterations as uncompressed SGD but improves the total communication by a factor of $\tilde{\Theta}(d^{\alpha} \varepsilon^{\beta})$. What is the ordered pair $(\alpha, \beta)$?

Consider the quasilinear heat equation with a reaction restricted to a half-line given by $u_t=(u^m)_{xx}+a(x) u^p$ for $x \in \mathbb{R}$ and $t>0$, with initial condition $u(x, 0) = u_0(x)$, where $m, p>0$, $a(x)=1$ for $x>0$, and $a(x)=0$ for $x<0$. Assume $u_0 \in L^1(\mathbb{R}) \cap L^\infty(\mathbb{R})$ is continuous and nonnegative. In terms of $m$, what is the exact value of the Fujita exponent $p_c$ for this equation?
What is the set of all solutions $(b, x, y, z)$ to the Diophantine equation $b^x + 2^y = (b-2)^z$ where $x, y, z$ are positive integers and $b$ is a positive odd integer?

Consider a network of $n$ identical Kuramoto oscillators in which each oscillator is coupled bidirectionally with unit strength to at least $\mu(n-1)$ other oscillators. Let $\mu_c$ be the critical value of the connectivity such that whenever $\mu > \mu_c$, the system is guaranteed to converge to the all-in-phase synchronous state for almost all initial conditions, but when $\mu < \mu_c$, there exist networks with other stable states. What is the precise numerical value of the best (lowest) upper bound for $\mu_c$ that can be obtained by a purely linear stability analysis?

Let $Q(n)$ be the number of ways to place $n$ mutually non-attacking queens on an $n \times n$ chessboard. By analyzing a two-stage randomized algorithm (which first uses a random greedy algorithm to construct an approximate toroidal configuration and then uses the method of absorbers), a lower bound of the form $Q(n) > \left( \left( 1 - o(1) \right) c n \right)^n$ was established. What is the explicit value of the constant $c$ obtained through this analysis?

Consider the directed configuration model, defined as a uniform directed graph on $n$ vertices with i.i.d. in- and out-degree pairs distributed as $(D^-, D^+)$ such that $\mathbb{E}[D^+] = \mathbb{E}[D^-] = \mu$, conditioned on having equal total in- and out-degrees. Assume the model is at the critical value for the emergence of a giant strongly connected component, which occurs when $\mathbb{E}[D^-D^+] = \mu$. Furthermore, assume the finite moment conditions $\mathbb{E}[(D^-)^i(D^+)^j] < \infty$ for all $i+j \leq 3$, as well as for $(i,j) = (1,3)$ and $(i,j) = (3,1)$. Under these conditions, the largest strongly connected components at criticality contain $\Theta(n^{\alpha})$ vertices and edges in probability, and their distances can be rescaled by $n^{-\alpha}$ such that the components (ranked by decreasing number of edges) converge in distribution to a sequence of finite strongly connected directed multigraphs with edge lengths. What is the exact value of the exponent $\alpha$?

What is the minimum number of vertices in a connected simple graph $G$ such that there exist two longest paths $C$ and $D$ in $G$ with $V(C) \neq V(D)$ for which the intersection of their vertex sets, $V(C) \cap V(D)$, is not a separator of $G$ (i.e., the induced subgraph on $V(G) \setminus (V(C) \cap V(D))$ is connected)?

Let $C_d^*$ denote the dual of the root polytope of type $C$ of dimension $d$. It has been proven that all roots of the Ehrhart polynomial of $C_d^*$ lie on a single vertical line in the complex plane. What is the real part of these roots?

Let $M \to B$ be a Riemannian submersion of compact smooth Riemannian manifolds, where $B$ is connected and the fiber is $Z$. For any compact smooth Riemannian manifold $N$, let $V_i(N)$ denote its $i$-th intrinsic volume (also known as Lipschitz-Killing curvature). For $\varepsilon > 0$, let $M_\varepsilon$ denote the manifold $M$ equipped with a modified Riemannian metric obtained by multiplying the original metric by $\varepsilon$ along the vertical directions and keeping it unchanged along the orthogonal horizontal directions. Express the limit $\lim_{\varepsilon\to +0} V_i(M_\varepsilon)$ in terms of $V_i(B)$ and the Euler characteristic $\chi(Z)$.

A Cayley graph $Cay(G,S)$ of a finite group $G$ with respect to a subset $S$ is said to be normal if the right regular representation of $G$ is normal in the automorphism group of $Cay(G,S)$. It is called a CI-graph if for any subset $T$ such that $Cay(G,S)\cong Cay(G,T)$, there exists $\alpha\in Aut(G)$ such that $S^\alpha=T$. A finite group $G$ is called a NCI-group if all normal Cayley graphs of $G$ are CI-graphs. It has been proven that a dihedral group of order $2n$ is a NCI-group if and only if $n$ is odd or $n$ belongs to a specific finite set $E$ of even positive integers. What is the set $E$?

Consider nonsingular Morse-Smale flows having exactly two limit cycles on closed manifolds. Let $c_T$ be the number of topological equivalence classes of such flows on the 2-dimensional torus $\mathbb{T}^2$, let $c_K$ be the number of topological equivalence classes of such flows on the Klein bottle, and let $c_S$ be the number of topological equivalence classes of such flows on the 3-dimensional sphere $\mathbb{S}^3$. What is the ordered triple $(c_T, c_K, c_S)$?

Consider the $b$-family of Camassa-Holm equations and the spectral instability of its peakon solutions. When the linearized operator around a peakon is extended to functions in $L^2(\mathbb{R})$, its spectrum generally covers a closed vertical strip of the complex plane. For what specific value of the parameter $b$ does this vertical strip shrink to the imaginary axis (with an additional pair of real eigenvalues existing due to projections to the peakon and its spatial translation)?

A fractional matching of a graph $G = (V, E)$ is a function $f: E 	o [0,1]$ such that for each $v \in V$, the sum of $f(e)$ over all edges $e$ incident to $v$ is at most 1. The size of a fractional matching $f$ is the sum of $f(e)$ over all $e \in E$. The fractional matching number $\alpha'_f(G)$ of a graph $G$ is defined as the maximum size of a fractional matching of $G$. For a fixed integer $n \ge 28$, consider the sum $\alpha'_f(G) + \alpha'_f(\overline{G})$ where $\overline{G}$ is the complement of $G$. Over all simple, undirected graphs $G$ of order $n$ such that neither $G$ nor $\overline{G}$ contains any isolated vertices, what is the exact minimum possible value of this sum as a function of $n$?

Let $S$ be a simplicial affine semigroup such that its semigroup ring $\mathbb{K}[S]$ is a $d$-dimensional Cohen-Macaulay ring. If the embedding dimension of $\mathbb{K}[S]$ is at most $d+2$, what is the maximum possible Cohen-Macaulay type of $\mathbb{K}[S]$?

Let $n$ and $k$ be integers with $4 \le k \le n-1$. Let a set of nodes $\mathcal{X}$ in the plane be $n$-independent, meaning each node $A \in \mathcal{X}$ has a fundamental polynomial of total degree at most $n$ (i.e., a bivariate polynomial $p$ of total degree at most $n$ such that $p(A) = 1$ and $p(B) = 0$ for all $B \in \mathcal{X} \setminus \{A\}$). Assume that the number of nodes is given by $\#\mathcal{X} = (n+1) + n + \dots + (n-k+5) + 3$. What is the maximum possible number of linearly independent algebraic curves of degree less than or equal to $k$ that pass through all the nodes of $\mathcal{X}$?

Let $L/K$ be any Galois extension of number fields such that $L\neq\mathbb{Q}$, and let $C$ be a conjugacy class in the Galois group of $L/K$. Ahn and Kwon proved that there exists a degree-one unramified prime $\mathfrak{p}$ of $K$ such that its Artin symbol $\sigma_{\mathfrak{p}}=C$ and its absolute norm satisfies $N \mathfrak{p} \le d_{L}^{B}$, where $d_L$ is the absolute value of the discriminant of $L$, with $B = 12\,577$. An improvement of this result established that this bound holds for a smaller integer value of $B$, obtained by using a refined version of Turán's power sum method giving a stronger Deuring-Heilbronn phenomenon alongside Fiori's numerical verification. What is this improved integer value of $B$?

Let $n, k,$ and $t$ be positive integers such that $k \ge n-1 \ge \max\left\{2t+1, 2\left\lceil \sqrt{2t+9/4}+1/2\right\rceil+3\right\}$. What is the maximum number of arcs in a simple directed graph on $n$ vertices such that for any ordered pair of vertices $(u,v)$, the number of directed walks of length $k$ from $u$ to $v$ is at most $t$?

A left-symmetric algebra (also known as a pre-Lie algebra) is an algebra over a field whose multiplication satisfies the identity $x(yz) - (xy)z = y(xz) - (yx)z$. Let $\mathcal{V}$ be the geometric variety of complex $4$-dimensional nilpotent left-symmetric algebras, considered as a subvariety of $\mathbb{C}^{64}$ (the space of all possible structure constants for a $4$-dimensional algebra). What is the dimension of the algebraic variety $\mathcal{V}$?

Let $A_n$ be a random $n \times n$ matrix with independent and identically distributed $\mathrm{Bernoulli}(d/n)$ entries, where $d>0$ is a constant. As $n \to \infty$, the reverse characteristic polynomial $\det(I_n - zA_n)$ converges in distribution to a random infinite product $\prod_{\ell = 1}^\infty(1-z^\ell)^{Y_\ell}$, where $Y_1, Y_2, \dots$ are independent Poisson random variables. What is the expected value of $Y_\ell$ as a function of $d$ and $\ell$?

Let $g^\ast(M)$ denote the cogirth (the size of a smallest cocircuit) of a matroid $M$ of non-zero rank, and let $E(M)$ be its ground set. There exists a constant $c$ such that $g^\ast(M) \leq c |E(M)|$ for all binary matroids $M$ of non-zero rank that do not simplify to a projective geometry, and this bound is tight (that is, equality is achieved for certain matroids, such as those that simplify to a Bose-Burton geometry). What is the exact value of the constant $c$?

A vertex coloring of a graph contains a square of period $p$ if there is a simple path in the graph whose sequence of vertex colors forms a word $WW$, where $W$ is a sequence of length $p$. Let $\pi_k(G)$ be the minimum number of colors needed to color the vertices of a graph $G$ such that no path in $G$ forms a square of period at least $k$. For a graph class $\mathcal{C}$, let $\pi_k(\mathcal{C}) = \sup_{G \in \mathcal{C}} \pi_k(G)$. What is the exact value of $\pi_5(\mathcal{T})$, where $\mathcal{T}$ is the class of all trees?

Let $S_k(x)$ be the sum $\sum_{n_1, \dots, n_k \le x} \frac{1}{\operatorname{lcm}(n_1, \dots, n_k)}$ over positive integers $n_1, \dots, n_k \in [1,x]$ for $k \ge 3$. Hilberdink, Luca, and T\u00f3th conjectured that $S_k(x)$ satisfies the asymptotic formula $S_k(x) = P_{2^k-1}(\log x) + O(x^{-\theta_k+\epsilon})$ for any $\epsilon > 0$, where $P_{2^k-1}$ is a polynomial of degree $2^k-1$ and $\theta_k$ is a specific closed-form function of $k$. What is this explicitly conjectured (and later proven) expression for $\theta_k$?

In the bandit version of phase retrieval, a learner sequentially chooses actions $(A_t)_{t=1}^n$ in the $d$-dimensional Euclidean unit ball. The expected reward at time $t$ is $\langle A_t, 	heta_\star \rangle^2$, where $\theta_\star \in \mathbb{R}^d$ with $\|\theta_\star\| \le 1$ is an unknown parameter vector. It is proven that the minimax cumulative regret in this problem is of the form $\tilde{\Theta}(d^a n^b)$, where the $\tilde{\Theta}$ notation hides logarithmic factors. What is the ordered pair $(a, b)$?

Let $n\ge 2$ and $k\ge 1$ be two integers. Let $M$ be an isometrically immersed closed $n$-submanifold of co-dimension $k$ that is homotopic to a point in a complete Riemannian manifold $N$, where the sectional curvature of $N$ is bounded above by a constant $\delta<0$. Let $H$ be the mean curvature vector of $M$ in $N$, and let $\lambda_1(M)$ be the first non-zero eigenvalue of the Laplacian on $M$. Let $V$ denote the volume of $M$ and let $E = \int_M |H|^2 \operatorname{dvol}$ be the total squared mean curvature of $M$. A theorem, which resolves a 1988 open problem by E. Heintze, establishes a sharp upper bound on $\lambda_1(M)$ as a function of $n$, $\delta$, $V$, and $E$. What is this upper bound?

The unparametrized Kepler orbits form a 3-parameter family of plane curves, consisting of all conics sharing a focus at a fixed point in the plane. Consider the group of local point symmetries of this family, that is, the local diffeomorphisms of the plane that preserve this 3-parameter family of curves. What is the dimension of this local symmetry group?

Let $\mathbf{N}_{\mathcal{P}}(n,H)$ denote the maximum number of subgraphs isomorphic to $H$ in an $n$-vertex planar graph. It is established that $\mathbf{N}_{\mathcal{P}}(n,C_{10}) \sim c n^5$ as $n \to \infty$. What is the exact value of the constant $c$?

Let $L$ be a lattice and $\lambda_k$ denote its $k$-th successive minimum. Let $v_1, \dots, v_n$ be a basis of $L$ which is reduced in the sense of Minkowski. Schürmann conjectured an upper bound for $k \geq 4$ of the form $\lvert v_k vert^2 \leq c_k \lambda_k^2$, which has been recently proven for $k = 6$ and $k = 7$. What is the explicit expression for $c_k$ as a function of $k$ posited by this conjecture?

Singmaster's conjecture asserts that for any natural number $t \geq 2$, the number of solutions to the equation $\binom{n}{m} = t$ for natural numbers $1 \leq m < n$ is bounded. A known theorem establishes this in the interior region $\exp(\log^{2/3+\varepsilon} n) \leq m \leq n-\exp(\log^{2/3 + \varepsilon} n)$ for any fixed $\varepsilon > 0$. Specifically, the theorem proves that when $t$ is sufficiently large depending on $\varepsilon$, there are at most $K$ solutions in this interior region, where $K$ is the explicit integer upper bound stated in the result. What is the value of $K$?

Let $a \geq 3$ be an integer. Consider the binary recurrence sequence defined by $u_0(a)=0$, $u_1(a)=1$, and $u_{n+2}(a)=a u_{n+1}(a)-u_n(a)$ for integers $n \geq 0$. An integer $x$ is called a near-square if $|x|$ is a perfect square or a prime number multiplied by a perfect square. Find the set of all pairs of integers $(n, a)$ with $5 \leq n \leq 18$ and $a \geq 3$ such that $u_n(a)$ is a near-square.

Consider the model of directed last-passage percolation on $\mathbb{Z}^2$ with i.i.d. exponential weights with mean 1. Let $\rho$ be a vector in the direction of the diagonal. Consider finite geodesics from $(0,0)$ to $n\rho$ in the limit as $n\to\infty$. The empirical distribution of the weights on the vertices along these geodesics converges almost surely to a limiting continuous distribution. What is the probability density function $f(x)$ of this limiting weight distribution?

An edge coloring of a simple graph $G$ is said to be proper rainbow-cycle-forbidding (PRCF) if no two incident edges receive the same color and for any cycle in $G$, at least two edges of that cycle receive the same color. A graph $G$ is defined to be PRCF-bad if it does not admit a PRCF edge coloring. While the Hoffman-Singleton graph provides an example of a PRCF-bad graph of girth 5, one can also construct PRCF-bad graphs of strictly larger girths. Specifically, by considering finite thick generalized polygons of sufficiently large order, one can prove the existence of PRCF-bad graphs for exactly four specific even girths strictly greater than 4. What is the set of these four girths?

Consider proper holomorphic maps from the unit ball in $\mathbb{C}^2$ into Cartan's classical domain of type IV in $\mathbb{C}^3$ that extend smoothly to some boundary point. Up to composition with automorphisms of the source and target, exactly how many maps are there in the complete classification of such maps?

Let $p, q > 1$. Consider a minimizer $u$ of the functional $J(v,\Omega) = \int_\Omega \left( |D v^+|^p + |D v^-|^q \right) dx$ over a domain $\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^n$, where $v^+ = \max(v, 0)$ and $v^- = \max(-v, 0)$. By considering inner variations, the weak formulation of the free boundary condition across the zero level surface $\partial \{u > 0\}$ can be formally represented by an equation of the form $|D u^-|^q = f(p,q) |D u^+|^p$. What is the expression for $f(p,q)$?

Let $\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^N$ be a suitable domain. For $s > 1/2$, let $u \in H^s_0(\Omega)$ be a minimizer of the regional fractional Laplacian energy functional $\mathcal{E}(u) := [u]_{H^s(\Omega)}^2 + \int_\Omega f u$, where $[\cdot]_{H^s(\Omega)}$ is the Gagliardo seminorm. Let $\delta$ denote the distance to the boundary $\partial\Omega$. The optimal global Schauder regularity up to the boundary for this Dirichlet problem establishes that $u/\delta^\gamma \in C^{1+\alpha}(\overline{\Omega})$ for $\alpha > 0$ strictly less than some threshold $\alpha_s \in (0, 1-s)$. What is the explicit value of the exponent $\gamma$ as a function of $s$?

Let $n \ge 2$ be an integer and let $M_n(\mathbb{C})$ be the space of $n \times n$ complex matrices. For $A,B \in M_n(\mathbb{C})$, define the Hilbert-Schmidt inner product $\langle A,B\rangle = \operatorname{tr}(A^\ast B)$, the associated Frobenius norm $\|A\| = \sqrt{\langle A,A\rangle}$, and the commutator $[A,B] = AB - BA$. Consider the real functional
\[ r(A,B) = \frac{1}{2}\Bigl(\langle [B,A],BA\rangle + \langle [B,A^\ast],BA^\ast \rangle \Bigr). \]
What is the exact value of the optimal constant $c_+$ (as a function of $n$) such that the inequality
\[ r(A,B) \le c_+ \|A\|^2 \|B\|^2 \]
holds for all $B \in M_n(\mathbb{C})$ and all traceless $A \in M_n(\mathbb{C})$?

In the study of the stability of the Caffarelli-Kohn-Nirenberg (CKN) inequality in the critical point setting, consider the inequality on $\mathbb{R}^N$ ($N \geq 3$) with parameters $a <  rac{N-2}{2}$, and $b$ such that either $b_{FS}(a) \leq b < a+1$ with $a < 0$ or $a \leq b < a+1$ with $a \geq 0$ and $a+b > 0$, where $b_{FS}(a)$ is the Felli-Schneider curve. Let $p =  rac{N+2(1+a-b)}{N-2(1+a-b)}$. Let $\Gamma(u) = \|	ext{div}(|x|^{-a}
abla u) + |x|^{-b(p+1)}|u|^{p-1}u\|_{(D^{1,2}_a)'}$. Let $\mathcal{Z}_0^
u$ be the set of $
u$-tuples of scaled radially symmetric extremal functions. For a nonnegative function $u \in D_a^{1,2}(\mathbb{R}^N)$ satisfying $(
u -  rac{1}{2})(C_{a,b,N}^{-1})^{ rac{p+1}{p-1}} < \|u\|_{D_{a}^{1,2}(\mathbb{R}^N)}^2 < (
u +  rac{1}{2})(C_{a,b,N}^{-1})^{ rac{p+1}{p-1}}$ and $\Gamma(u) \leq \delta$ for some $\delta > 0$ sufficiently small, it is known that the stability bound for the distance to $\mathcal{Z}_0^
u$ in the space $D^{1,2}_a$ takes the form $	ext{dist}_{D_a^{1,2}}(u, \mathcal{Z}_0^
u) \lesssim \Gamma(u)^q$ in the parameter regime $1 < p < 2$ and $
u \geq 2$. What is the exact exponent $q$ as a function of $p$?
Let $X$ be a smooth complex Fano 4-fold admitting a small elementary contraction. What is the proven upper bound on its Picard number $ho(X)$?

Consider the planar symplectic ensemble of $2N$ complex eigenvalues of non-Hermitian random matrices with potentials of Mittag-Leffler type given by $Q(\zeta) = |\zeta|^{2\lambda} - \frac{2c}{N}\log|\zeta|$, where $\lambda>0$ and $c>-1$. In the limit $N\to\infty$, the limiting kernel at the origin obeys a linear differential equation of a certain fractional order. What is this fractional order as a function of $\lambda$?

The bilinear Hilbert--Carleson operator is defined for $a>0$ by
$$ BC^{a}(f,g)(x):= \sup_{\lambda\in {\mathbb R}} \Big|\int f(x-t)\, g(x+t)\, e^{i\lambda t^a} \, \frac{dt}{t} \Big|. $$
In the non-resonant case $a\in (0,\infty)\setminus\{1,2\}$, it is a theorem that the operator $BC^a$ extends continuously from $L^p({\mathbb R})\times L^q({\mathbb R})$ into $L^r({\mathbb R})$ whenever $\frac{1}{p}+\frac{1}{q}=\frac{1}{r}$ with $1<p,q\leq\infty$ and $r_0<r<\infty$. What is the value of the critical infimum exponent $r_0$ for this range of boundedness?

The class of quasi-graphic matroids is a minor-closed class of matroids that contains both lifted-graphic and frame matroids. Up to isomorphism, there are exactly two $9$-connected excluded minors for the class of quasi-graphic matroids. Both of these excluded minors are uniform matroids, which can be denoted as $U_{r_1, n_1}$ and $U_{r_2, n_2}$ (where $U_{r,n}$ represents the uniform matroid of rank $r$ on an $n$-element set). Assuming $r_1 < r_2$, what is the ordered tuple $(r_1, n_1, r_2, n_2)$?

Let $k$ be a field of characteristic two and let $k_{\mathrm{alg}}$ be its algebraic closure. For an element $\alpha \in k_{\mathrm{alg}}$ of degree $n \ge 1$ over $k$, let $s(\alpha)$ denote the minimal integer $m$ such that there exists an $m \times m$ symmetric matrix with entries in $k$ that has $\alpha$ as an eigenvalue. What is the maximum possible value of $s(\alpha)$ over all such fields $k$ and elements $\alpha$ of degree $n$?

Let $\mathcal{A}^{\mathbb{R}}$ denote the mod 2 $\mathbb{R}$-motivic Steenrod algebra, and let $\mathcal{A}^{\mathbb{R}}(1)$ be its finite subalgebra generated by $\mathrm{Sq}^1$ and $\mathrm{Sq}^2$. How many different $\mathcal{A}^{\mathbb{R}}$-module structures can be given to $\mathcal{A}^{\mathbb{R}}(1)$ extending its canonical left $\mathcal{A}^{\mathbb{R}}(1)$-module structure?

Let $q=2^m$. Consider the narrow-sense BCH code $\mathcal{C}_{(q,q+1,4,1)}$ of length $q+1$. It has been established that the codewords of the minimum or the second minimum weight in these codes support infinite families of $4$-designs or $3$-designs. What is the weight $w$ of the codewords of the next adjacent weight, which are proven to support $4$-designs when $m \geqslant 5$ is odd and $3$-designs when $m \geqslant 4$ is even?

Let $k$ and $r$ be integers with $k \ge r \ge 2$ and let $\gamma > 0$. Suppose $G$ is a $k$-partite graph on $n$ vertices with ordered partition $\mathcal{P} = (V_1, \ldots, V_k)$ in which $|V_i| \le n/r$ for all $i \in [k]$ and $\delta_{\mathcal{P}}(G) \ge 1-1/r+\gamma$, where $\delta_{\mathcal{P}}(G) = \min_{i \ne j \in [k]} \frac{\min\{ \deg_G(v, V_j) : v \in V_i \}}{|V_j|}$. What is the maximum integer $p$, as a function of $r$, such that for all sufficiently large $n$, every such graph $G$ is guaranteed to contain the $p$-th power of a Hamiltonian cycle?

Consider heat transport across an open, ordered harmonic chain of charged particles with transverse degrees of freedom, connected to heat baths at its two ends, in the presence of a uniform magnetic field. In the thermodynamic limit, at small frequency $\omega$, the effective transmission scales with frequency as $\omega^\alpha$ for fixed boundary conditions. What is the exact value of the exponent $\alpha$?

The 1-cusped quotient of the hyperbolic space $\mathbb{H}^3$ by a certain tetrahedral Coxeter group $\Gamma_*$ has minimal volume among all non-arithmetic cusped hyperbolic 3-orbifolds, and as such it is uniquely determined. In standard Coxeter notation for linear diagrams, this group is denoted by $\Gamma_* = [p, q, r]$. Assuming $p \le r$, what is the ordered tuple of integers $(p, q, r)$?

Consider the solution $h_\varepsilon(t,x)$ to the one-dimensional Kardar-Parisi-Zhang (KPZ) equation under weak noise scaling with the narrow wedge initial data, where the spacetime white noise is multiplied by a small parameter $\sqrt{\varepsilon}$ and $\varepsilon \to 0$. Let $\Phi(\lambda) = -\lim_{\varepsilon \to 0} \varepsilon \log \mathbb{P}[h_\varepsilon(2, 0) + \log \sqrt{4\pi} \geq \lambda]$ denote the one-point upper tail large deviation rate function. As $\lambda \to \infty$, $\Phi(\lambda)$ exhibits a power law behavior of the form $\Phi(\lambda) \sim c \lambda^\alpha$ for some constants $c > 0$ and $\alpha$. What is the exact value of the exponent $\alpha$?

Let $S_n$ denote the set of permutations of $\{1, 2, \dots, n\}$. A permutation in $S_n$ is called evil-avoiding if it avoids the classical patterns $2413$, $4132$, $4213$, and $3214$. What is the total number of evil-avoiding permutations in $S_n$ as a function of $n$?

Consider the semi-linear defocusing wave equation $u_{t t}-\Delta u=-|u|^{p-1} u$ in $d$-dimensional space $\mathbb{R}^d$. For radial solutions in the sub-conformal case, scattering can be established if the initial data $(u_0, u_1)$ satisfy the finite weighted energy condition
$$E_\kappa(u_{0}, u_{1})=\int_{\mathbb{R}^{d}}\left(|x|^\kappa+1\right)\left(\frac{1}{2}\left|\nabla u_{0}(x)\right|^{2}+\frac{1}{2}\left|u_{1}(x)\right|^{2}+\frac{1}{p+1}\left|u_{0}(x)\right|^{p+1}\right) d x < +\infty.$$
What is the precise value of the exponent $\kappa$, expressed in terms of $d$ and $p$, that arises in this scattering criterion?

Let $n \ge 2$. Let $\Omega$ be a homogeneous function of degree zero on $\mathbb{R}^n \setminus \{0\}$ such that its integral on the unit sphere $\mathbb{S}^{n-1}$ vanishes, and $\Omega \in L\log L(\mathbb{S}^{n-1})$. Let $T_\Omega$ be the singular integral operator on $\mathbb{R}^n$ associated with the rough kernel $\Omega$, defined by $T_\Omega f(x) = \mathrm{p.v.} \int_{\mathbb{R}^n} \frac{\Omega(y)}{|y|^n} f(x-y) \,dy$. For $0 \le f \in L^1(\mathbb{R}^n)$, it is known that $\lim_{\lambda \to 0^+} \lambda |\{x \in \mathbb{R}^n : |T_\Omega f(x)| > \lambda\}| = C \|\Omega\|_{L^1(\mathbb{S}^{n-1})} \|f\|_{L^1(\mathbb{R}^n)}$ for some constant $C$ depending on $n$. What is $C$ as a function of $n$?

Let $n \ge 2$ be an integer. Consider a set $S \subset \mathbb{R}^d$ consisting of $n$ points. For any vantage point $v \in \mathbb{R}^d$, we can order the points of $S$ by their Euclidean distance from $v$. Let $O(S)$ denote the number of distinct strict linear orderings of $S$ induced by all vantage points $v \in \mathbb{R}^d$ for which the distances to the points of $S$ are pairwise distinct. Over all possible choices of $n$-point sets $S$ and all dimensions $d \ge 1$, what is the minimum possible value of $O(S)$ as a function of $n$?

Let $T_n$ be a random Bienaymé-Galton-Watson tree of size $n$ generated by an offspring distribution $\xi$ with mean $1$ and nonzero finite variance. Assume that $p_1 = \mathbf{P}(\xi=1) > 0$. The leaf-height of a node $v \in T_n$ is defined as the minimum distance (shortest path length) from $v$ to a leaf in the subtree rooted at $v$. Let $H_n$ be the maximum leaf-height over all nodes in $T_n$. As $n \to \infty$, $H_n / \ln n$ converges in probability to a constant $c$. Express the constant $c$ in terms of $p_1$.

Consider a $2\times n$ rectangular grid composed of $1\times 1$ squares. Let $N(n, k)$ be the number of ways to divide the grid into exactly $k$ pieces by cutting only along the edges between the squares. It is known that for any fixed $k$, $N(n, k)$ is given by a polynomial in $n$ for sufficiently large $n$. What is the degree of this polynomial as a function of $k$?

Suppose we choose a permutation $\pi$ uniformly at random from the symmetric group $S_n$. Let $\mathsf{runsort}(\pi)$ be the permutation obtained by sorting the ascending runs of $\pi$ into lexicographic order. As $n\to\infty$, the measures corresponding to the scaled plots of $\mathsf{runsort}(\pi)$ on the unit square $[0,1]^2$ converge with probability 1 to a limit permuton. The support of this limit permuton is exactly the set of points $\{(x,y)\in[0,1]^2 : x \leq f(y)\}$ for some continuous function $f(y)$. What is the closed-form expression for $f(y)$?

Let $G$ be a connected graph with vertex set $V$ of order $n \ge 2$, and let $k$ be a positive integer. A set $D \subseteq V$ is a distance-$k$ dominating set of $G$ if, for each vertex $u \in V \setminus D$, there exists a vertex $w \in D$ such that $d(u,w) \le k$. Let $d_k(x,y) = \min\{d(x,y), k+1\}$. A set $R \subseteq V$ is a distance-$k$ resolving set of $G$ if, for any pair of distinct $x,y \in V$, there exists a vertex $z \in R$ such that $d_k(x,z) \neq d_k(y,z)$. The distance-$k$ dimension $\dim_k(G)$ is the minimum cardinality of a distance-$k$ resolving set of $G$. The distance-$k$ location-domination number $\gamma_L^k(G)$ is the minimum cardinality of a set $S \subseteq V$ that is both a distance-$k$ dominating set and a distance-$k$ resolving set of $G$. A sharp upper bound for $\gamma_L^k(G)$ established for all such graphs is given by an expression depending only on $n$ and $\dim_k(G)$. What is this upper bound?

Let $G$ be a finite group. Let $V(G)$ denote the set of all values appearing in the complex character table of $G$, meaning $V(G) = \{\chi(g) \mid \chi \in \operatorname{Irr}(G), g \in G\}$, where $\operatorname{Irr}(G)$ is the set of all irreducible complex characters of $G$. What is the minimum possible cardinality of $V(G)$ across all non-solvable finite groups $G$?

Consider the large time behavior of solutions of the compressible viscoelastic system around a motionless state in a three-dimensional whole space. If the initial perturbation belongs to $W^{2,1}$, is sufficiently small in $H^4\cap L^1$, and satisfies suitable low-frequency conditions, it is shown that the solutions grow in time in the $L^1$ norm at the same rate as $t^\alpha$ due to diffusion wave phenomena. What is the value of the exponent $\alpha$?
Up to combinatorial equivalence, how many types of convex bodies can form a twofold translative tiling in the three-dimensional Euclidean space $\mathbb{E}^3$?

Let $\Sigma$ be the covariance matrix of a prescribed anisotropic Gaussian distribution in $\mathbb{R}^d$. Consider the class of non-symmetric Fokker-Planck equations with linear drift and a constant diffusion matrix $D$ satisfying $\text{Tr}(D) \le d$ that admit this Gaussian as their unique equilibrium. In an $L^2$-analysis, among such equations that allow for an exponential decay estimate towards the equilibrium with a multiplicative constant arbitrarily close to its infimum of 1, what is the maximum achievable exponential decay rate?

Assuming the Strong Exponential Time Hypothesis, what is the infimum of the set of constants $c > 0$ such that there exists an algorithm to solve the Odd Cycle Transversal problem in time $\mathcal{O}(c^k \cdot n^{\mathcal{O}(1)})$ on an input graph given as a clique-width expression of size $n$ and width $k$?

Consider the problem of finding an $\varepsilon$-stationary point for a nonconvex-strongly-concave minimax optimization problem. An accelerated Adaptive Gradient Descent Ascent (VR-AdaGDA) method, based on a momentum-based variance reduction technique, achieves a gradient complexity of $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(\kappa^p \varepsilon^q)$ without using large batches, where $\kappa$ is the condition number. What is the ordered pair $(p, q)$?

Consider the two-dimensional Dirac operator $A_0 = -i (\sigma_1 \partial_x + \sigma_2 \partial_y) + m \sigma_3$ acting on $L^2(\mathbb{R}^2, \mathbb{C}^2)$, where $m > 0$ and $\sigma_1, \sigma_2, \sigma_3$ are the standard Pauli matrices. Let $A_\eta$ be the self-adjoint realization of $A_0$ perturbed by an electrostatic $\delta$-shell interaction of strength $\eta \in \mathbb{R}$ supported on a straight line. There exist critical values of the interaction strength $\eta$ such that the continuous spectrum of $A_\eta$ inside the spectral gap $(-m, m)$ of $A_0$ collapses abruptly to a single point. What is the set of these critical values of $\eta$?

Suppose $\hat{X}$ and $\hat{Y}$ are operators satisfying the commutator relation $[\hat{X},\hat{Y}] = u\hat{X} + v\hat{Y} + c\mathcal{I}$, where $u,v,c$ are scalars and $\mathcal{I}$ is the identity operator. The exponential of their sum can be exactly decomposed into the right-sided closed form
$$
e^{\hat{X}+\hat{Y}} = e^{\hat{X}}e^{\hat{Y}}e^{g_r(u,v)[\hat{X},\hat{Y}]}
$$
where $g_r(u,v)$ is a scalar function of $u$ and $v$. For $u \neq v$ and $u,v \neq 0$, what is the exact expression for $g_r(u,v)$?

In computability theory, for an integer $n \ge 1$, an $n$-REA set is defined to be *properly $n$-REA* if it is not Turing equivalent to any $(n-1)$-REA set. It has been proved that every properly $1$-REA set can be extended to a properly $2$-REA set, and that every properly $2$-REA set can be extended to a properly $3$-REA set. What is the smallest positive integer $n$ for which there exists a properly $n$-REA set that cannot be extended to a properly $(n+1)$-REA set?

Let $\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a convex planar domain with sufficiently large eccentricity. Consider the second eigenfunction of the Laplacian on $\Omega$ with Dirichlet boundary conditions. What is the exact number of critical points of this eigenfunction in $\Omega$?

Consider the ultraviolet completion of the pure $T\bar{T}$ deformation of the Ising model (which has an infra-red central charge $c_{IR}= 1/2$) obtained by including an infinite number of additional irrelevant perturbations. It has been found that there are two such UV completions, one of which corresponds to the tri-critical Ising model, and both of which possess $\mathcal{N}=1$ supersymmetry that is broken in the renormalization group flow to low energies. What are the ultra-violet central charges $c_{UV}$ of these two completions? Provide your answer as an ordered pair $(c_1, c_2)$ such that $c_1 < c_2$.

Let $C$ be Klein's plane quartic curve, $J$ its Jacobian variety, and $G$ the full automorphism group of $J$ (which has order 336). The quotient variety $J/G$ can be represented as the quotient $\mathbb{C}^3/\tilde{G}$, where $\tilde{G}$ is a complex crystallographic group generated by affine complex reflections. It is known that the quotient $J/G$ is a strongly simply connected variety with the same singularities as a weighted projective space $\mathbb{P}(w_0, w_1, w_2, w_3)$. Assuming the weights are positive integers that satisfy $w_0 \le w_1 \le w_2 \le w_3$ and $\gcd(w_0, w_1, w_2, w_3) = 1$, what is the 4-tuple of weights $(w_0, w_1, w_2, w_3)$?

Let $q > 2$ be a prime power. Consider an optimal three-weight cyclic code $\mathcal{C}$ over the finite field $\mathbb{F}_q$ having length $q+1$, dimension $3$, and nonzero weights $q-1$, $q$, and $q+1$. What is the minimum Hamming distance of its dual code $\mathcal{C}^{\perp}$?

Let $G = \mathbb{Z}_{4u}\times \mathbb{Z}_{8v}$, where $u$ and $v$ are odd positive integers. A $(G, \{4,5\}, 1)$ difference packing is a family of subsets (called blocks) of $G$, each of size 4 or 5, such that the multiset of all differences $a-b$ (for distinct $a,b$ in the same block) contains every non-zero element of $G$ at most once. The difference packing is called balanced if the number of blocks of size 4 is equal to the number of blocks of size 5. What is the largest possible total number of blocks in a balanced $(G, \{4,5\}, 1)$ difference packing, as a function of $u$ and $v$?

Let $k$ be a field of characteristic zero. The non-abelian cohomology $\mathrm{H}^2_{\mathrm{nab}}(k, k^n)$ associated to the abelian Jordan algebras $k$ and $k^n$, which classifies all extensions of $k$ by $k^n$, is identified with the set of all matrices $D \in M_n(k)$ satisfying a specific polynomial equation $P(D) = 0$. What is the polynomial $P(x) \in \mathbb{Z}[x]$ of minimal degree that defines this set, chosen such that its coefficients are coprime integers and its leading coefficient is positive?

Let $V$ be a $4$-dimensional vector space over $\mathbb{C}$. The set of all bilinear maps $\mu: V \times V \to V$ that equip $V$ with the structure of a nilpotent right alternative algebra forms an algebraic variety in $\operatorname{Hom}(V \otimes V, V)$. What is the dimension of this variety?

A generalization of the Yao-Yao partition theorem establishes that for any positive integers $t$ and $d$, and any finite measure in $\mathbb{R}^d$ absolutely continuous with respect to the Lebesgue measure, there exists an equipartition of the measure into $N$ convex regions such that every hyperplane misses the interior of at least $t$ regions. What is the specific expression for the number of regions $N$ as a function of $t$ and $d$?

A Latin square of order $n$ is an $n\times n$ array containing $n$ distinct integer symbols exactly once in each row and column. The adjacent distance between two horizontally or vertically adjacent cells containing values $a$ and $b$ is defined as their distance in the cyclic group $\mathbb{Z}_n$, i.e., $\min(|a-b|, n-|a-b|)$ where the symbols are identified with $0, 1, \dots, n-1$. The inner distance of a Latin square is the minimum of the adjacent distances over all pairs of adjacent cells in the square. For $n \ge 3$, as a function of $n$, what is the maximum possible inner distance of an $n \times n$ Latin square?

For two graphs $X$ and $Y$ with vertex sets $V(X)$ and $V(Y)$ of the same cardinality $n,$ the friends-and-strangers graph $\mathsf{FS}(X,Y)$ is a graph whose vertices are the bijections from $V(X)$ to $V(Y),$ and two bijections $\sigma$ and $\tau$ are adjacent if they agree everywhere except at two vertices $a,b\in V(X)$ such that $a$ and $b$ are adjacent in $X$ and $\sigma(a)$ and $\sigma(b)$ are adjacent in $Y.$ Let $r$ be an even positive integer, and let $K_{r,r}$ denote the complete bipartite graph with parts of size $r$. Let $d^*_{r,r}$ be the minimum integer such that for any spanning subgraph $X$ of $K_{r,r}$ satisfying minimum degree $\delta(X)\ge d^*_{r,r},$ the graph $\mathsf{FS}(X,K_{r,r})$ has exactly two connected components. What is the exact value of $d^*_{r,r}$ as a closed-form expression in terms of $r$?

Consider a harmonic chain of oscillators in the presence of a random magnetic field, with the ends of the chain connected to heat baths. Let $\lambda(\omega)$ be the Lyapunov exponent of the system at normal mode frequency $\omega$. In the low-frequency limit as $\omega \to 0$, the Lyapunov exponent behaves as $\lambda(\omega) \sim \omega^\alpha$. What is the exact value of the exponent $\alpha$ when the mean of the random magnetic field is zero ($\langle B \rangle = 0$)?

Let $p$ be a prime. In the context of higher-order Fourier analysis over the vector space $\mathbb{F}_p^n$, what is the largest integer $k$, as a function of $p$, such that classical polynomials suffice for the inverse theorem for the Gowers $U^k$-norm (meaning that any bounded function on $\mathbb{F}_p^n$ with a large $U^k$-norm must correlate with a classical polynomial)?

Let $K$ be a number field with ring of integers $\mathcal{O}_K$, and let $E \subseteq \mathcal{O}_K$ be a set with positive upper Banach density $d^*(E) = \delta > 0$. Let $\{p_1, \dots, p_k\} \subseteq K[x]$ be a jointly intersective family of linearly independent $\mathcal{O}_K$-valued polynomials. For any $\varepsilon > 0$, it is proven that the set of $n \in \mathcal{O}_K$ such that
\[ d^* \left( \{ x \in \mathcal{O}_K : \{x, x + p_1(n), \dots, x + p_k(n)\} \subseteq E \} \right) > f(\delta) - \varepsilon \]
is syndetic. What is the expression for $f(\delta)$ in terms of $\delta$ and $k$?

Let $E$ be a finite set and $\sigma: 2^E \to 2^E$ be a closure operator satisfying the anti-exchange property. Two sets $X, Y \subseteq E$ are called cospanning if $\sigma(X) = \sigma(Y)$, and the equivalence class of $X$ under this relation is denoted by $[X]_\sigma$. Let $ex(X) = \{x \in X \mid x \notin \sigma(X \setminus \{x\})\}$ denote the set of extreme points of $X$. The equivalence class $[X]_\sigma$ forms an interval $[A, B] = \{Y \subseteq E \mid A \subseteq Y \subseteq B\}$ in the poset of subsets of $E$ ordered by inclusion. What is the ordered pair $(A, B)$ in terms of $X$, the function $ex$, and the closure operator $\sigma$?

A Boolean function is a $k$-SAT function if it can be expressed as a $k$-CNF formula. A Boolean function is unate if it becomes monotone after negating some subset of its variables. The Bollobás--Brightwell--Leader conjecture states that for each fixed $k \ge 2$, a $1-o(1)$ fraction of all $k$-SAT functions on $n$ variables are unate. Equivalently, the conjecture states that the total number of $k$-SAT functions on $n$ variables is $(1+o(1)) 2^{g(n,k)}$ as $n \to \infty$. What is the closed-form expression for $g(n,k)$ in terms of $n$ and $k$?

A position $p$ in a word $w$ is critical if the minimal local period at $p$ is equal to the global period of $w$. Let $\eta(w)$ denote the number of critical points of $w$, and let $|w|$ denote the length of $w$. For sufficiently long square-free words $w$ over a three-letter alphabet, there exists an integer $k$ such that $\eta(w) \le |w| - k$, and this bound is exactly reached by infinitely many such words. What is the value of $k$?

A frame $\{f_i\}_{i=1}^m$ in $\mathbb{R}^n$ is called norm retrievable if the absolute values of the inner products $\{|\langle x, f_i \rangle|\}_{i=1}^m$ uniquely determine the norm $\|x\|$ for all $x \in \mathbb{R}^n$. What is the maximal integer $m$, as a function of $n$, such that the set of norm retrievable frames of size $m$ in $\mathbb{R}^n$ is not dense in the set of all $m$-element sets of vectors in $\mathbb{R}^n$?

Consider the weighted matching and matroid intersection problems. A solution is defined to be lexicographically maximal if it includes as many elements of the largest weight as possible, and subject to this condition, as many elements of the second largest weight as possible, and so on. Suppose the distinct positive weights present in an instance are $w_1 > w_2 > \dots > w_k > 0$, and define the minimum weight ratio as $\alpha = \min_{1 \le i < k} \frac{w_i}{w_{i+1}}$. If $1 \le \alpha < 2$, what is the tight approximation factor (i.e., the worst-case ratio of the total weight of a lexicographically maximal solution to the optimal total weight) achieved by a lexicographically maximal solution, as a function of $\alpha$?

Let $R$ be an associative ring with identity which is not commutative. Assume that any non-zero commutator $v \in R$ satisfies two conditions: $v^2$ is in the center $Z(R)$ of $R$, and $v$ is not a zero-divisor. Assume also that the characteristic of $R$ is not 2. Under these conditions, $R$ has no zero-divisors, and thus its center $Z(R)$ is an integral domain. Let $K$ be the field of fractions of $Z(R)$, and let $Q$ be the localization of $R$ at its center $Z(R)$ (i.e., the central localization where all non-zero elements of $Z(R)$ are inverted). What is the dimension of $Q$ as a vector space over $K$?

The Cauchy problem for the Fornberg-Whitham equation is locally well-posed in the Besov spaces $B^s_{p,r}$ for $1 \leq p, r \leq +\infty$ when the regularity index $s$ is strictly greater than a critical threshold $s_c(p)$. Furthermore, local well-posedness also holds in the critical Besov spaces $B^{s_c(p)}_{p,1}$ for $1 \leq p < +\infty$. What is the closed-form expression for the critical threshold $s_c(p)$?

Consider the mixing and diffusion properties of passive scalars driven by generic rough shear flows. When the spatial regularity of the flow is measured in the Besov-Nikolskii scale $B^\alpha_{1, \infty}$ with $\alpha \in (0, 1)$, enhanced dissipation holds with a sharp rate $r(
u) \sim 
u^ eta$ as the diffusion coefficient $
u 	o 0$. What is the exact value of the exponent $ eta$ as a function of $\alpha$?

Consider the passive scalar advection-diffusion equation $\partial_t \theta - \Delta \theta + b \cdot \nabla \theta = 0$, where $b$ is a divergence-free drift in $n$ spatial dimensions. Local boundedness, a single-scale Harnack inequality, and upper bounds on fundamental solutions are satisfied for steady (time-independent) drifts $b$ under the sharp condition that $b \in L^p_x$ for $p > p_c$. What is the critical exponent $p_c$ as a function of $n$?

Let $r \geq 2$, and let $n$ and $k$ be integers satisfying $k \leq \frac{r-1}{r}n$. What is the minimum number of $r$-wise intersecting families into which the family of all $k$-element subsets of an $n$-element set can be partitioned? (A family of subsets is $r$-wise intersecting if any collection of at most $r$ subsets in it has a common point.)

Consider an incompressible non-Newtonian flow problem with power-law rheology in $d$ space dimensions, where $d \in \{2,3\}$. Weak solutions to this model are known to exist when the power-law index $r$ is strictly greater than a critical threshold $f(d)$. A stabilised finite element method, using a continuous piecewise linear approximation for the velocity field, a piecewise constant approximation for the pressure, and stabilisation via pressure jumps, has been proven to converge for the entire range $r > f(d)$. What is the exact expression for this critical threshold $f(d)$?

Let $p$ be a fixed prime, and let $I(p^n)$ denote the number of isomorphism classes of finite rings of order $p^n$. As $n \to \infty$, the number of isomorphism classes is given by $I(p^n) = p^{\alpha(n)}$, where $\alpha(n) = C n^3 + O(n^{5/2})$ for a real constant $C$. What is the exact value of the constant $C$?

Consider the one-dimensional geometric graph model where graphs are formed by $n$ points in the interval $[0,1]$, with an edge between any two points if and only if their distance is at most $r$. Let $S(n, r)$ be the number of distinct unlabeled graphs that can be realized in this way. For a fixed $r \in (0,1]$, it is established that $S(n, r) = \Theta(a(r)^{2n})$ as $n \to \infty$ for a specific positive function $a(r)$. What is the exact closed-form expression for $a(r)$?

Consider a game between two players, Ann and Ben, who build a word together by alternately appending a letter from an alphabet of size $k$ to the end of a shared initially empty word, with Ann playing first. Ben wins the game if a square of length at least $4$ (i.e., a contiguous subword of the form $ww$ where $w$ has length at least $2$) is created. Ann wins if she can ensure the word grows arbitrarily long without such a square ever being created. What is the minimum integer $k$ for which Ann has a winning strategy?

Let $p \geq 5$ be a prime and let $\Gamma$ be a connected symmetric graph of valency $p$ that admits a quasi-semiregular automorphism (an automorphism fixing exactly one vertex such that its remaining cycles have the same length). It has been proven that $\Gamma$ must satisfy one of two conditions. The first condition is that $\Gamma$ is a normal $N$-cover of a $T$-arc-transitive graph of valency $p$ admitting a quasi-semiregular automorphism, where $T$ is a non-abelian simple group and $N$ is a nilpotent group. The second condition is that $\Gamma$ is a connected Cayley graph $\operatorname{Cay}(M,S)$ such that $S$ is an orbit of involutions, and $M$ is an $r$-group admitting a fixed-point-free automorphism of order $p$. What is the value of the prime $r$?

Let $S$ be a non-trivial Severi-Brauer surface over an arbitrary field $\Bbbk$ of characteristic $0$. Let $G \subset \operatorname{Aut}(S)$ be a finite group. The quotient variety $S/G$ is $\Bbbk$-rational if and only if the order $|G|$ is divisible by a specific prime number $p$. What is the value of $p$?
Let $C$ be a regular trifolium in the Euclidean plane. For a generic offset distance $d>0$, what is the algebraic degree of the offset curve of $C$?

A vertex coloring of a graph $G$ (not necessarily proper) is called distinguishing if no non-identity automorphism of $G$ preserves it. The distinguishing threshold of $G$, denoted by $\theta(G)$, is the minimum number $c$ such that every $c$-coloring of $G$ is distinguishing. Let $n, k, i$ be integers such that $1 \le i \le k < n/2$. The generalized Johnson graph $J(n,k,i)$ is the graph whose vertices are all $k$-element subsets of $\{1,\ldots , n\}$, where two vertices $A$ and $B$ are adjacent if and only if $|A\cap B|=k-i$. What is the distinguishing threshold $\theta(J(n,k,i))$ in terms of $n$ and $k$?

A Kakeya set in $\mathbb{F}_q^n$ is a subset $K \subseteq \mathbb{F}_q^n$ containing a line in every direction. Let $D(q, n)$ be the minimum possible density $|K|/q^n$ of a Kakeya set in $\mathbb{F}_q^n$. What is the value of $\inf_q D(q, n)$ (where the infimum is taken over all prime powers $q$) as a function of $n$?

Let $X$ denote the space of orientation-compatible almost complex structures on the 6-dimensional sphere $S^6$, equipped with the standard $C^\infty$ topology. What is the cardinality of the fundamental group $\pi_1(X)$?

A complex unit gain graph is a triple $G^\phi=(G, \mathbb{T}, \phi)$ consisting of a simple graph $G$ with oriented edges $\overrightarrow{E}$, the set of unit complex numbers $\mathbb{T}=\{z\in \mathbb{C}: |z| = 1\}$, and a gain function $\phi: \overrightarrow{E}\rightarrow \mathbb{T}$ such that $\phi(e_{i,j})=\phi(e_{j,i})^{-1}$. Let $A(G^\phi)$ be the adjacency matrix of $G^\phi$, and let $p(G^\phi)$ be the number of positive eigenvalues of $A(G^\phi)$. Let $m(G)$ denote the matching number of $G$, and let $c(G)$ denote the cyclomatic number of $G$. What is the tight upper bound on $p(G^\phi)$ in terms of $m(G)$ and $c(G)$?
Let $\mathcal{A} = \{10a + 11b \mid a, b \in \mathbb{Z}, \gcd(a,b)=1, a \geq 1, b \geq 2a+1\}$. What is the largest integer $M$ such that $M \notin \mathcal{A}$?

Consider an infinite system of identical bosons interacting via a positive-type pair potential in $d \geq 3$ dimensions. Let $N$ be the number of particles. It has been established that Bose-Einstein condensation occurs in this system if and only if the diverging cycle lengths increase at least as fast with $N$ as $N^{x}$. What is the exponent $x$ as a function of the dimension $d$?

Let $G$ be a connected graph with vertex set $V(G)$ of order $n \ge 2$, and let $d(x,y)$ denote the length of a shortest $x-y$ path in $G$. Let $k$ be a positive integer. For any $x,y \in V(G)$, let $d_k(x,y)=\min\{d(x,y), k+1\}$ and let $R_k\{x,y\}=\{z\in V(G): d_k(x,z) \neq d_k(y,z)\}$. A real-valued function $g:V(G) \rightarrow[0,1]$ is a $k$-truncated resolving function of $G$ if $\sum_{s\in R_k\{x,y\}} g(s) \ge 1$ for any distinct $x, y\in V(G)$. The fractional $k$-truncated metric dimension $\dim_{k,f}(G)$ of $G$ is the minimum of $\sum_{s\in V(G)} g(s)$ over all such functions $g$. What is the maximum possible value of $\dim_{k,f}(G)$ over all connected graphs $G$ of order $n \ge 2$?

Let $(X_1, X_2)$ be a pair of independent Ornstein-Uhlenbeck processes satisfying $dX_i(t) = -\theta X_i(t) dt + dW_i(t)$ with $X_i(0) = 0$ for $i \in \{1,2\}$, where $W_1, W_2$ are independent standard Brownian motions and $\theta > 0$. Define Yule's nonsense correlation statistic on the time interval $[0,T]$ as
$$ \rho(T) := \frac{Y_{12}(T)}{\sqrt{Y_{11}(T)}\sqrt{Y_{22}(T)}}, $$
where for $i,j \in \{1,2\}$,
$$ Y_{ij}(T) := \int_{0}^{T} X_i(u) X_j(u) du - T \bar{X}_i \bar{X}_j, \quad \text{and} \quad \bar{X}_i := \frac{1}{T} \int_{0}^{T} X_i(u) du. $$
As $T \to \infty$, $\rho(T)T^{1/2}$ converges in law to a centered normal distribution. What is the asymptotic variance of $\rho(T)T^{1/2}$?

The Ablowitz-Ladik hierarchy possesses a local tri-Hamiltonian structure. Consider the associated bihamiltonian structure whose dispersionless limit coincides with the bihamiltonian structure defined on the jet space of the Frobenius manifold associated with the Gromov-Witten invariants of local $\mathbb{CP}^1$. What is the constant value of the central invariants of this bihamiltonian structure?

Let $f(z) = \sum_{n=1}^\infty a_f(n)q^n$ be a holomorphic cuspidal newform with even integral weight $k\geq 2$, squarefree level $N$, trivial nebentypus, and no complex multiplication. For all primes $p$, define $\theta_p\in [0,\pi]$ such that $a_f(p) = 2p^{(k-1)/2}\cos \theta_p$. Let $\mu_{\textrm{ST}}(I) = \frac{2}{\pi}\int_I \sin^2 \theta\; d\theta$ for $I\subseteq [0,\pi]$. Define $\pi_{f,I}(x) := \#\{ p \leq x : p \nmid N, \theta_p\in I\}$, and $\pi(x) := \# \{ p \leq x \}$. Utilizing the automorphy of symmetric-power $L$-functions due to Newton and Thorne, an explicit bound has been established which states that for $x \geq 3$,
$$ \left| \frac{\pi_{f,I}(x)}{\pi(x)} - \mu_{\textrm{ST}}(I)\right| \leq C \frac{\log((k-1)N \log{x})}{\sqrt{\log{x}}} $$
What is the specific numerical constant $C$ obtained in this explicit bound?

Let the bipartite Ramsey number $B(n_1,n_2,\ldots,n_t)$ be defined as the least positive integer $b$ such that any coloring of the edges of the complete bipartite graph $K_{b,b}$ with $t$ colors will result in a monochromatic copy of $K_{n_i,n_i}$ in the $i$-th color, for some $i$ where $1 \leq i \leq t$. What is the exact value of $B(2,2,3)$?

For the isospin $I=3/2$ pion-nucleon ($\pi N$) scattering amplitude $T^{I=3/2}(s,t)$, there exists an accumulation point of poles on the second sheet of the complex $s$ plane. What is the exact value of this accumulation point $s$ in terms of the nucleon mass $m_N$ and the pion mass $m_\pi$?

Consider words $w$ over a binary alphabet. A word $w$ is called overlap-free if it does not contain any factor (contiguous subword) of the form $uvuvu$, where $u$ and $v$ are words and $u$ is nonempty. A square in a word is a factor of the form $xx$, where $x$ is a nonempty word. Let $|w|$ denote the length of $w$, and let $M(w)$ denote the number of distinct positions in $w$ (i.e., boundaries between consecutive letters) that are the middle position of at least one square in $w$. What is the maximum value of $2M(w) - |w|$ over all overlap-free binary words $w$?

Let $P(b)$ denote the number of non-negative integer solutions to the linear Diophantine equation $\sum_{i=1}^{n} a_i x_i=b$, where $a_1,a_2,\dots,a_n$ are coprime positive integers and $b$ is a non-negative integer. Let $A = \prod_{i=1}^n a_i$, $S = \sum_{i=1}^n a_i$, and $M = \operatorname{lcm}(a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_n)$. Let $r$ be the remainder of $b$ modulo $M$, and define $s = \left\lfloor n - \frac{S + r}{M} \right\rfloor$. If $s=0$, there is an explicit formula for $P(b)$. What is this explicit formula for $P(b)$? Express your answer purely as a mathematical expression in terms of the variables $M, n, A$, and $b$. Use $\lfloor \cdot \rfloor$ for the floor function and $\binom{x}{y}$ for the binomial coefficient.

Let a queue with respect to a vertex ordering of a graph be a set of edges such that no two edges nest (i.e., have their endpoints ordered in an ABBA-pattern). The local queue number of a graph is the smallest integer $k$ for which there is a vertex ordering and a partition of the edges into queues such that each vertex has incident edges in at most $k$ queues. It has been proven that the local queue number of the complete graph $K_n$ on $n$ vertices is $c n \pm O(1)$ for some constant $c$. What is the exact value of the constant $c$?

A graph $X$ is said to be "unstable" if the direct product $X \times K_2$ (also called the canonical double cover of $X$) has automorphisms that do not come from automorphisms of its factors $X$ and $K_2$. It is "nontrivially unstable" if it is unstable, connected, and nonbipartite, and no two distinct vertices of $X$ have exactly the same neighbors. What is the smallest even integer $n \ge 8$ for which there does not exist a nontrivially unstable circulant graph of order $n$?

Let $m \ge 2$ be a fixed integer. Consider the following statement $S(k)$ depending on a positive integer $k$: "For every Banach lattice $E$, any non-zero scalars $\lambda_1, \ldots, \lambda_k$, and any pairwise independent linear functionals $\varphi_1, \ldots, \varphi_k$ on $E$, the functional $x \mapsto \sum_{j=1}^k \lambda_j (\varphi_j(x))^m$ is orthogonally additive on $E$ if and only if for each $j \in \{1, \ldots, k\}$, either $\varphi_j$ or $-\varphi_j$ is a lattice homomorphism." What is the largest integer $k$, as an expression in terms of $m$, such that the statement $S(k)$ is universally true?

Let $I = I_\Delta$ be the Stanley-Reisner ideal of a simplicial complex $\Delta$. Let $\delta = \lim_{n\to\infty} \operatorname{reg}(I^{(n)})/n$, and $b = \max\{\operatorname{reg}(I_\Gamma) \mid \Gamma \text{ is a subcomplex of } \Delta \text{ with } \mathcal{F}(\Gamma) \subseteq \mathcal{F}(\Delta)\}$, where $\mathcal{F}$ denotes the set of facets. What is the sharp upper bound on the regularity of the $n$-th symbolic power, $\operatorname{reg}(I^{(n)})$, valid for all $n \geqslant 1$?

A connected symmetric $v_3$ configuration is an incidence structure consisting of $v$ points and $v$ blocks, where each block contains exactly 3 points, each point is contained in exactly 3 blocks, any two distinct blocks intersect in at most one point, and the configuration is not the union of two configurations on distinct point sets. A triangle in such a configuration is a set of three blocks that mutually intersect in three distinct points (for example, blocks $\{a,b,f\}$, $\{a,c,e\}$, and $\{b,c,d\}$ form a triangle). What is the largest integer $v$ such that there does not exist any triangle-free connected symmetric $v_3$ configuration?

Consider the long time behavior of the solutions to the one-dimensional Burgers-FKPP equation $u_t + \beta uu_x = u_{xx} + u - u^2$ with advection of strength $\beta \in \mathbb{R}$ and an initial condition that limits to 1 at $-\infty$, 0 at $+\infty$, and is steeper than the minimal speed traveling wave. This equation exhibits a transition from pulled to pushed front behavior at the critical advection strength $\beta_c=2$. At this critical value $\beta = 2$, the asymptotic expansion of the front location $m_2(t)$ as $t\to+\infty$ takes the form $m_2(t) = 2t - \alpha \log(t) + x_\infty + o(1)$, reflecting the "pushmi-pullyu" nature of the front. What is the exact value of the logarithmic correction coefficient $\alpha$?

Consider the vector Dyson equation $-1/m=z+Sm$, where $S$ is a block matrix consisting of $n^2$ blocks (an $n \times n$ block structure). Suppose $S$ has a block staircase structure with $(n-1)$ different critical zero blocks below the strictly positive anti-diagonal, and all elements right above the anti-diagonal are strictly positive. As $E$ tends to $0$, it is proven that the self-consistent density of states $\rho(E)$ behaves asymptotically as $|E|^\alpha$. What is the value of the exponent $\alpha$ in terms of $n$?

Let $M$ be a unitary $S^1$-manifold with only isolated fixed points such that $M$ is not a boundary. The Kosniowski conjecture asserts the strict inequality $k \chi(M) > \dim M$ for a specific integer $k$, where $\chi(M)$ is the Euler characteristic of $M$. What is the value of this integer $k$?

Let $n \geq 3$ be an integer. In the $n$-dimensional Boolean lattice $2^{[n]}$, what is the maximum number of $2$-chains (pairs of sets $A, B$ such that $A \subsetneq B$) in an $\mathbf{N}$-free subfamily of $2^{[n]}$, where $\mathbf{N}$ is the poset on 4 distinct elements $\{p_1,p_2,q_1,q_2\}$ whose strict relations are $p_1 < q_1$, $p_2 < q_1$, and $p_2 < q_2$?

In the model theory of W$^*$-probability spaces (von Neumann algebras equipped with a faithful normal state) formulated in continuous logic, the class of type III$_1$ factors forms a $\forall_2$-axiomatizable class. For a fixed $\lambda \in (0,1)$, it can be shown using a continuous logic version of Keisler's Sandwich theorem that the class of type III$_\lambda$ factors is $\forall_k$-axiomatizable. What is the smallest positive integer $k$ for which the class of type III$_\lambda$ factors is $\forall_k$-axiomatizable?

Let $P$ be an algebraically closed field of characteristic $p>2$, and let $G_n = Sp_{2n}(P)$ be the symplectic group of degree $2n$ over $P$. Let $\omega_1, \dots, \omega_n$ denote the fundamental weights of $G_n$. Let $\phi_n^2$ be the irreducible representation of $G_n$ with highest weight $\frac{p-1}{2}\omega_n$. What is the dimension of $\phi_n^2$ as a function of $p$ and $n$?

A set $D$ of vertices of a graph $G$ is called a dissociation set if each vertex of $D$ has at most one neighbor in $D$. The dissociation number of $G$, denoted $diss(G)$, is the maximum cardinality of a dissociation set in $G$. The Kneser graph $K_{n,k}$ is the graph whose vertices correspond to the $k$-element subsets of an $n$-element set, where two vertices are adjacent if and only if their corresponding sets are disjoint. In terms of $k$, what is the exact value of the dissociation number $diss(K_{2k+1,k})$ for an integer $k \geq 2$?

An extension of the Matomäki-Radziwiłł theorem allows for estimating averages of divisor-bounded multiplicative functions in short intervals. As an application, it has been proven that the Hooley $\Delta$-function has an average value $\gg \log\log X$ in typical short intervals of length $(\log X)^{c+\eta}$, where $\eta>0$ is a fixed parameter. What is the precise numerical value of the constant $c$?

Consider the 2D Navier-Stokes equations around the Poiseuille flow $(y^2,0)$ on $\mathbb{T}\times\mathbb{R}$ with small kinematic viscosity $\nu>0$. According to the linear hypocoercivity analysis, the $x$-dependent modes of the solution to the linearized problem undergo the enhanced dissipation effect with a rate proportional to $\nu^{\beta}$. What is the value of the exponent $\beta$?

Let $C_k$ denote the cycle graph on $k$ vertices. Let $m$ and $n$ be odd integers such that at least one of them is equal to $3$ or $5$. What is the matching book thickness of the Cartesian product $C_m \square C_n$?

Let $C$ be a smooth projective curve of genus $g\ge2$ and let $N$ be the moduli space of stable rank $2$ vector bundles on $C$ with a fixed determinant of odd degree. The BGMN conjecture predicts a specific semi-orthogonal decomposition of the bounded derived category of $N$. A semi-orthogonal sequence of blocks in the bounded derived category of $N$ has been constructed, where each block is equivalent to the bounded derived category of the $i$-th symmetric power of $C$ for some $0 \le i \le g-1$. Let $b_i$ denote the number of blocks corresponding to the $i$-th symmetric power of $C$ in this constructed sequence. Assuming the subcategory generated by these constructed blocks has a trivial semi-orthogonal complement, what is the total number of blocks $\sum_{i=0}^{g-1} b_i$ in this semi-orthogonal decomposition of the bounded derived category of $N$, given as a closed-form expression in terms of $g$?

Let $\textbf{k} := (k_1,\ldots,k_s)$ be a sequence of natural numbers. For a graph $G$, let $F(G;\textbf{k})$ denote the number of colourings of the edges of $G$ with colours $1,\dots,s$ such that, for every $c \in \{1,\dots,s\}$, the edges of colour $c$ contain no clique of order $k_c$. Let $F(n;\textbf{k})$ denote the maximum of $F(G;\textbf{k})$ over all graphs $G$ on $n$ vertices. For the sequence $\textbf{k}=(3,3,3,3,3,3,3)$ of length $s=7$, it has been proven that for sufficiently large $n$, the unique $n$-vertex graph $G$ that attains the maximum $F(n;\textbf{k})$ is a complete balanced $r$-partite graph. What is the value of $r$?

For a connected graph $G=(V,E)$, the Steiner distance $d_G(X)$ of a set of vertices $X \subseteq V$ is the minimum size (number of edges) among all connected subgraphs of $G$ whose vertex set contains $X$. The $k$-Steiner distance matrix $D_k(G)$ of $G$ is the matrix whose rows and columns are indexed by the $k$-element subsets of $V$, where the $(X_1,X_2)$-entry is given by $d_G(X_1 \cup X_2)$. What is the rank over $\mathbb{R}$ of the $2$-Steiner distance matrix $D_2(G)$ for a caterpillar graph $G$ on $N$ vertices with $p$ pendant vertices?

Let $d \in \mathbb{N}$. Let $\mathbb{T}^d_n$ be the $d$-dimensional torus of volume $n$, equipped with the metric inherited from the Euclidean metric in $\mathbb{R}^d$. We construct the online nearest neighbor random tree $G_n$ by embedding $n$ vertices consecutively, uniformly at random and independently in $\mathbb{T}^d_n$, and letting each vertex after the first connect to its nearest previously embedded neighbor. As $n \to \infty$, the graph diameter of $G_n$ is typically of the form $(c+o(1))\log n$, where $c$ is a constant independent of $d$. What is the exact value of the constant $c$?

The generalized trust-region subproblem is a real-valued nonconvex quadratic optimization problem with a single quadratic constraint. What is the maximum possible number of local nonglobal minimizers that a two-dimensional generalized trust-region subproblem can possess?

Let $X \subset \mathbb{P}^N$ be a projective manifold of dimension $n$ and degree $d$. Suppose that the tangent bundle $T_X$ is an Ulrich bundle with respect to the polarization $\mathcal{O}_X(1)$ induced by the embedding. What is the set of all possible pairs $(n, d)$ for such a manifold $X$?

Let $B_4$ be the braid group on $4$ strands, and let $Z$ be its center, generated by $\theta$. Let $\tau$ and $\Delta$ be the standard fourth and square roots of $\theta$ in $B_4$, respectively. Let $G = \langle \tau, \Delta \rangle$ be the subgroup of $B_4$ generated by $\tau$ and $\Delta$. The quotient group $G/Z$ is isomorphic to the free product of two finite cyclic groups $\mathbb{Z}_n * \mathbb{Z}_m$. Assuming $n \ge m$, what is the ordered pair $(n, m)$?

The Winger pencil is a pencil of genus ten sextic plane curves invariant under the standard action of the icosahedral group on the projective plane. The Jacobian of a smooth curve in this pencil contains the tensor product of an elliptic curve with a certain integral representation of the icosahedral group. It has been proven that the monodromy on this part of the homology is the full congruence subgroup $\Gamma_1(N) \subset \mathrm{SL}_2(\mathbb{Z})$ for some positive integer $N$, and that the base of the pencil is identified with the associated modular curve. What is the value of $N$?

What is the value of the universal constant $c$ such that for any semisimple Frobenius manifold, a tau-symmetric bihamiltonian deformation of its Principal Hierarchy admits an infinite family of linearizable Virasoro symmetries if and only if all the central invariants of the corresponding deformation of the bihamiltonian structure are equal to $c$?

Absolute model companionship (AMC) is a strict strengthening of model companionship defined as follows: for a theory $T$, $T_{\exists\vee\forall}$ denotes the logical consequences of $T$ which are boolean combinations of universal sentences; $T^*$ is the AMC of $T$ if it is model complete and $T_{\exists\vee\forall}=T^*_{\exists\vee\forall}$. Let $\mathsf{Form}_\tau$ be the set of first order $\tau$-formulae. For $A\subseteq \mathsf{Form}_\tau$, let $\tau_A$ be the expansion of $\tau$ adding atomic relation symbols $R_\phi$ for all formulae $\phi$ in $A$, and let $T_{\tau,A}$ be the $\tau_A$-theory asserting that each $\tau$-formula $\phi(\vec{x})\in A$ is logically equivalent to the corresponding atomic formula $R_\phi(\vec{x})$. For a $\tau$-theory $T$, $T+T_{\tau,A}$ is the partial Morleyization of $T$ induced by $A\subseteq \mathsf{Form}_\tau$. It has been shown that a definable version of the identity $2^{\aleph_0}=\aleph_n$ is the unique solution to the continuum problem which can be in the AMC of a partial Morleyization of the $\in$-theory $\mathsf{ZFC}$ + "there are class many supercompact cardinals". What is the value of the integer $n$?

Consider a closed Riemannian manifold $(M^{m}, g)$ of dimension $m \ge 3$ whose volume is the same as the standard round sphere $(S^{m}, g_{round})$. Let $Rc$ denote the Ricci curvature tensor of $g$, and let $\{Rc-(m-1)g\}_{-}$ denote the negative part of the tensor $Rc-(m-1)g$.
