Let $G_{6,3}=\langle a_0, \dots, a_5 \mid a_{i}^{3}=1, a_{i} a_{i+1}= a_{i+1} a_{i}, i \in \mathbb{Z}/6\mathbb{Z}\rangle$ be a hyperbolic group. It is known that there exists a discrete, convex cocompact and faithful representation $\rho$ of $G_{6,3}$ into $\mathbf{PU}(2,1)$. The 3-orbifold at infinity of $\rho(G_{6,3})$ is a closed hyperbolic 3-orbifold, whose underlying space is the 3-sphere and whose singular locus is the $\mathbb{Z}_k$-coned chain-link $C(p, q)$. What is the ordered triple $(k, p, q)$?
What is the infimum of the Busemann-Hausdorff area over all Finsler reversible $2$-tori with unit systole?

The Gan-Loh-Sudakov conjecture, which has been proven, determines the exact maximum number of cliques of size $t$ (where $t \ge 3$) that can exist in a simple graph on $n$ vertices with maximum degree at most $\Delta$. Given $n$ and $\Delta$, we can write $n = a(\Delta+1) + b$ where $a$ and $b$ are integers such that $a \ge 0$ and $0 \le b \le \Delta$. What is this maximum number of cliques in terms of $a, b, \Delta$, and $t$?

In the study of dependency equilibria in game theory, the Spohn variety of a game is defined by linear constraints on conditional probabilities, which correspond to determinantal quadrics in the tensor entries. For a generic 2-person game where the first player has 3 strategies and the second player has 2 strategies (a $3 	imes 2$ game), the Spohn variety is known to be a del Pezzo surface. What is the degree of this del Pezzo surface?

Let $G$ be a finite group, and let $G^- = \{ g \in G \mid \langle g \rangle \text{ is not a maximal cyclic subgroup of } G \}$. Suppose that the subgroup generated by $G^-$, denoted $\langle G^- \rangle$, is a proper subgroup of $G$. It is proven that the quotient group $G/\langle G^- \rangle$ must be isomorphic to either (1) a $p$-group of exponent $p$ for some prime $p$, (2) a Frobenius group whose Frobenius kernel is a $p$-group of exponent $p$ and whose Frobenius complement has prime order $q$ for distinct primes $p$ and $q$, or (3) a specific non-abelian finite simple group. What is this finite simple group?

Let ${\sf sbn}(G)$ denote the strict bramble number of a finite simple graph $G$, defined as the maximum order of a strict bramble in $G$. A strict bramble is a collection of pairwise-intersecting connected subgraphs of $G$, and its order is the minimum size of a vertex set that intersects all subgraphs in the collection. The class of graphs satisfying ${\sf sbn}(G) \leq 2$ is closed under taking minors. How many graphs are there in the minor-obstruction set (the set of minor-minimal forbidden minors) for the class of graphs with ${\sf sbn}(G) \leq 2$?

Consider the fractional Schrödinger-Poisson system
\[
\begin{cases} 
\varepsilon^{2s}(-\Delta)^{s}u+u+\phi u=u^p, & \text{in } \Omega,\\
(-\Delta)^{s}\phi=u^2, & \text{in } \Omega,\\
u=\phi=0, & \text{in } \mathbb{R}^N\setminus \Omega, 
\end{cases}
\]
where $s\in(0,1)$, $2s < N \leq 6s$, $p\in \left(1,\frac{N+2s}{N-2s}\right)$, $\Omega$ is a bounded domain in $\mathbb{R}^N$ with Lipschitz boundary, $(-\Delta)^{s}$ is the fractional Laplacian operator, and $\varepsilon$ is a small positive parameter. By using the Lyapunov-Schmidt reduction method, there exists a single peak solution $(u_\varepsilon,\phi_\varepsilon)$ such that the peak of $u_\varepsilon$ is in the domain but near the boundary. As $\varepsilon$ tends to $0$, this solution exhibits boundary concentration, where the peak concentrates at an approximate distance $\varepsilon^\alpha$ away from the boundary $\partial\Omega$. What is the exact value of the exponent $\alpha$?

Consider the numerical integration ${\rm INT}_d(f)=\int_{\mathbb{B}^{d}}f(x)w_\mu(x)dx$ for the weighted Sobolev classes $BW^{r}_{p,\mu}$ and the weighted Besov classes $BB_\tau^r(L_{p,\mu})$ in the randomized case setting, where $w_\mu$ ($\mu\ge0$) is the classical Jacobi weight on the ball $\mathbb{B}^d$, $1\le p\le \infty$, $r>(d+2\mu)/p$, and $0<\tau\le\infty$. The order of the optimal quadrature errors in the randomized case setting, as a function of the number of information evaluations $n$, is $\Theta(n^\alpha)$. What is the exact exponent $\alpha$ in terms of $r, d,$ and $p$? (If your answer involves the positive part function $(x)_+$, write it as $\max(x,0)$).

Let $T$ be a convex $n$-gon in the plane, and let $X_T(\omega)$ denote its width along the direction $\omega \in [0, 2\pi)$. Assuming $\omega$ is a uniformly distributed random variable, define the deviation rate of the width of $T$ as
$$ \delta(X_T) = \frac{\sqrt{\mathbb{E}[X_T^2] - (\mathbb{E}[X_T])^2}}{\mathbb{E}[X_T]}. $$
For a given integer $n \ge 2$ which is not a power of $2$, what is the minimum possible value of $\delta(X_T)$ among all convex $n$-gons $T$?

For two given nonnegative integers $h$ and $k$, an $L(h,k)$-edge labeling of a graph $G$ is an assignment of integer labels from $\{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ to the edges of $G$ such that any two edges sharing a common vertex receive labels that differ by at least $h$, and any two edges that do not share a vertex but both share a vertex with a third common edge receive labels that differ by at least $k$. The span $\lambda'_{h,k}(G)$ is the minimum integer $n$ such that $G$ admits an $L(h,k)$-edge labeling. Let $T_4$ denote the infinite regular square grid graph (whose vertex set is $\mathbb{Z}^2$ with edges between vertices at Euclidean distance 1). What is the exact value of $\lambda'_{1,2}(T_4)$?

A code $\mathcal{C} \subseteq \{0, 1, 2\}^n$ is said to be trifferent with length $n$ if for any three distinct elements of $\mathcal{C}$ there exists a coordinate in which they all differ. What is the maximum cardinality of a trifferent code with length $6$?

Consider the sample space of labeled trees on $n$ vertices, chosen uniformly at random. Let $X_d$ denote the number of vertices with degree $d$ in the tree. It is a known result that for any two different degrees, the random variables are jointly asymptotically normal as $n \to \infty$. However, they are generally not asymptotically independent. There is exactly one pair of degrees $(d_1, d_2)$ with $d_1 < d_2$ for which $X_{d_1}$ and $X_{d_2}$ are asymptotically independent. What is this pair $(d_1, d_2)$?

For $\lambda\in(0,1/3]$, let $C_\lambda$ be the middle-$(1-2\lambda)$ Cantor set in $\mathbb R$. Given $t\in(0,1)\setminus\{1/3\}$, define the set
$$ \Lambda(t) := \{\lambda\in(0,1/3]: C_\lambda\cap(C_\lambda+t)\neq\emptyset\} $$
For any $\beta\in[0,1]$, define the level set
$$ \Lambda_\beta(t) := \left\{\lambda\in\Lambda(t): \dim_H(C_\lambda\cap(C_\lambda+t)) = \dim_P(C_\lambda\cap(C_\lambda+t)) = \beta\frac{\log 2}{-\log \lambda}\right\} $$
where $\dim_H$ and $\dim_P$ are the Hausdorff and packing dimensions, respectively. It is known that the Hausdorff dimension of $\Lambda_\beta(t)$ depends only on $\beta$. What is the Hausdorff dimension of $\Lambda_\beta(t)$ as a function of $\beta$?

Consider the iterated function system (IFS) on $\mathbb{R}^2$ given by $f_{\vec{q}}(\vec{z})=\frac{\vec{z}+\vec{q}}{\beta}$ for $\vec{q}\in\{(0,0),(1,0),(0,1)\}$, with attractor $S_\beta$. For $1 < \beta \le 3/2$, $S_\beta$ is a triangle and all $\beta$-expansions of a point $\vec{z} \in S_\beta$ can be generated by a random map $K_\beta$ defined on $\{0,1\}^\mathbb{N}\times\{0,1,2\}^\mathbb{N}\times S_\beta$. The map $K_\beta$ has a unique invariant measure of maximal entropy for $1 < \beta \le \beta_*$, where $\beta_*$ is the sharp critical parameter. What is the monic minimal polynomial of $\beta_*$ over $\mathbb{Q}$? (Provide the polynomial in the variable $x$.)

Let $\overline{p}(n)$ denote the overpartition function, which counts the number of ways to partition an integer $n$ into parts where the first occurrence of each part may be overlined. Let $\Delta$ denote the forward difference operator with respect to $n$, defined by $\Delta f(n) = f(n+1) - f(n)$. For any non-negative real number $\alpha$, evaluate the limit
$$ \lim_{n \to \infty} n^{5/2} \Delta^2 \left( \log \sqrt[n]{\frac{\overline{p}(n)}{n^\alpha}} \right). $$

The upper density of an infinite graph $G$ with $V(G) \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is defined as $\overline{d}(G) = \limsup_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{|V(G) \cap \{1,\ldots,n\}|}{n}$. Let $K_{\mathbb{N}}$ be the infinite complete graph with vertex set $\mathbb{N}$. What is the maximum real number $c$ such that every $3$-edge-colouring of $K_{\mathbb{N}}$ is guaranteed to contain a monochromatic path $P$ with upper density $\overline{d}(P) \ge c$?

Let $n \geq 2$ be an integer. What is the optimal lower bound $v_{n, n-1}$ of the canonical volume for minimal projective $n$-folds of general type over an algebraically closed field of characteristic zero that are canonically fibered by $1$-folds (i.e., having canonical dimension $n-1$)?

Let $M=(E,r)$ be a system with a ground set $E$ of size $m$ and a function $r: 2^E\to \mathbb{Z}_{\geq 0}$ satisfying $r(S)\leq \min(r(E),|S|)$ for every set $S\subseteq E$. The Tutte polynomial of $M$ is defined as
$$T_M(x,y):=\sum_{S\subseteq E}(x-1)^{r(E)-r(S)}(y-1)^{|S|-r(S)}$$
Write the polynomial as $T_M(x,y)=\sum_{i,j}t_{ij}x^iy^j$ and let $r=r(E)$. For any integer $h \geq 0$, evaluate the sum
$$\sum_{i=0}^h\sum_{j=0}^{h-i}\binom{h-i}{j}(-1)^jt_{ij}$$
in terms of $h$, $m$, and $r$.

Let $D$ be a digraph. The fractional arboricity $\gamma(D)$ is defined as $\gamma(D) = \max_{H \subseteq D, |V(H)|>1} \frac{|A(H)|}{|V(H)|-1}$. For positive integers $k$ and $d$, let $f(k,d)$ be the supremum over all real numbers $c$ such that every digraph $D$ with maximum in-degree $\Delta^-(D) \leq k+1$ and fractional arboricity $\gamma(D) \leq c$ can be decomposed into $k+1$ branchings $B_1, \ldots, B_{k+1}$ such that the maximum out-degree of the last branching satisfies $\Delta^+(B_{k+1}) \leq d$. What is the exact value of $f(k, d)$ for $d \leq k$?

Consider the repulsive Euler-Poisson equations with a non-zero background, modeling cold plasma oscillations. For radially symmetric solutions in multidimensional spaces ($d \ge 2$), it is established that the solutions blow up in finite time for almost all initial data (except possibly for simple waves), with the exception of exactly one spatial dimension $d$. What is the value of this exceptional spatial dimension $d$?

Let $p$ be a prime number. In the theory of higher-level differential modules and opers in positive characteristic $p$, a duality theorem establishes a correspondence between dormant $\mathrm{PGL}_n$-opers of level $N > 0$ on a pointed smooth curve (where $0 < n < p^N$) and dormant $\mathrm{PGL}_m$-opers of level $N$ on the same curve. What is the value of $m$ given as a function of $p, N,$ and $n$?

Let $e$ and $f$ be two Ising vectors in a moonshine type vertex operator algebra over $\mathbb{R}$ with a positive definite invariant bilinear form, and let $\mathbb{C}\mathrm{VA}(e, f)$ be the complexified vertex operator subalgebra generated by $e$ and $f$. If $\mathbb{C}\mathrm{VA}(e, f)$ is isomorphic to the $6A$-algebra $\mathcal{U}_{6A}$ constructed by Lam, Yamada, and Yamauchi, what is the value of the inner product $\langle e, f \rangle$?

A finite graph $G$ is called a pentagraph if every cycle in $G$ has length at least five, and every induced cycle of odd length in $G$ has length exactly five. Let $\chi(G)$ denote the chromatic number of $G$. What is the maximum value of $\chi(G)$ over all finite pentagraphs $G$?

A multigraph is $(k,d)$-edge colourable if its edge set can be partitioned into $k$ subgraphs of maximum degree at most $d$. Let $f(\Delta, d)$ be the minimum integer $k$ such that every multigraph with maximum degree $\Delta$ is guaranteed to be $(k,d)$-edge colourable. For an odd integer $d$, what is the exact value of $f(\Delta, d)$?

Let $\Pi_n$ be a random permutation in the symmetric group $S_n$ drawn from the Mallows distribution, where the probability of a permutation $\pi$ is proportional to $q^{\text{inv}(\pi)}$ for a parameter $q > 1$, and $\text{inv}(\pi)$ denotes the number of inversions. Let $C_1(\Pi_n)$ denote the number of 1-cycles (fixed points) of $\Pi_n$. As $n \to \infty$ through the even integers, $C_1(\Pi_n)$ converges in distribution to a discrete limit. Let $\mu(q)$ be the expected value of this limiting distribution. What is the exact value of $\lim_{q \downarrow 1} \mu(q)$?

Consider Bernoulli site percolation on the Uniform Infinite Planar Triangulation (UIPT). At criticality, the probability that the root cluster has at least $n$ vertices decays as $n^{-\alpha}$ (up to slowly varying factors) for some critical exponent $\alpha > 0$. What is the exact value of $\alpha$?

Consider the Infinite Ising Planar Triangulation, which is the infinite volume limit of random planar triangulations sampled with a probability proportional to the Boltzmann weight of an Ising configuration on their vertices. In this model, the root spin cluster is finite almost surely for critical and supercritical temperatures, but infinite with positive probability for subcritical temperatures. What is the exact value of the percolation critical exponent $\beta$ characterizing the probability of the root spin cluster being infinite?
Let $f(m,n)$ be the number of primitive lattice triangulations of an $m\times n$ rectangle. What is the exact value of the limit $\lim_{n\to\infty} f(2,n)^{1/n}$?

Let $\mathcal{QLE}$ denote the total quasi-local energy for a closed space-like $2$-surface in a spacetime with gravity coupled to a U(1) gauge field, defined as the Wang-Yau quasi-local energy plus the additional term arising from the gauge field coupling. For a Kerr-Newman black hole spacetime, under the assumption of small angular momentum, what is the value of $\mathcal{QLE}$ evaluated on the outer horizon? Express your answer in terms of the black hole's irreducible mass $M_{irr}$ and the electric potential energy $V_Q$.

Let $(M, g, J)$ be a compact Hermitian surface (of complex dimension 2). The Gauduchon line of canonical Hermitian connections is parameterized by a real number $t$ as $
abla^t =  rac{1+t}{2}
abla^C +  rac{1-t}{2}
abla^B$, where $
abla^C$ is the Chern connection and $
abla^B$ is the Bismut connection. Suppose $(M, g, J)$ is not Kähler and has pointwise constant holomorphic sectional curvature with respect to $
abla^t$. What is the set of all possible values for the parameter $t$?

The dichromatic number of a directed graph is the minimum number of colors needed to color its vertices such that each color class induces an acyclic subgraph. An oriented graph is a directed graph with at most one arc between any pair of vertices (and no loops). An oriented graph $D$ is called $3$-dicritical if its dichromatic number is at least $3$, but the removal of any arc from $D$ results in an oriented graph with a dichromatic number of at most $2$. What is the minimum possible number of arcs in a $3$-dicritical oriented graph?

The spectrum $\omega(G)$ of a finite group $G$ is the set of orders of its elements. Let $Sz(q)$ denote the simple exceptional Suzuki group over the field of $q$ elements. Up to isomorphism, exactly how many finite groups $G$ have the same spectrum as the direct square $Sz(32) \times Sz(32)$?

Let $\mathfrak h_t$ be the KPZ fixed point started from any initial condition that guarantees $\mathfrak h_t$ has a maximum at every time $t$ almost surely. Let $\mathcal T_k \subset (0, \infty)$ denote the set of times when $\max \mathfrak h_t$ is achieved at exactly $k$ points. What is the almost sure Hausdorff dimension of $\mathcal T_3$?

Let $A$ be an arbitrary planar convex body. It is a known fact that $A$ permits an inscribed affine-regular hexagon $H_A$. Let $c$ be the center of $H_A$. There exists a minimal real number $r > 0$ such that, for every planar convex body $A$ and every affine-regular hexagon $H_A$ inscribed in $A$, the centroid of $A$ is guaranteed to lie within the homothetic image of $H_A$ with center $c$ and ratio $r$. What is the exact value of this optimal ratio $r$?

Let $P$ be a Beurling generalized prime number system satisfying Axiom A with parameter $	heta$, and let $\Delta_P(x) := \psi_{P}(x)-x$ be the remainder term, where $\psi_{P}(x)$ is the generalized von Mangoldt summatory function. Let $\rho_0$ be a given zero of the corresponding Beurling zeta function $\zeta_P$ with $\Re \rho_0 > \theta$ and $\Im \rho_0 \neq 0$. What is the exact optimal constant $C$ such that for any $\varepsilon > 0$, $\Delta_P(x)$ is guaranteed to exhibit oscillations of magnitude at least $(C - \varepsilon) \frac{x^{\Re \rho_0}}{|\rho_0|}$ in any large enough interval for any such number system, while for any $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists a Beurling number system where $|\Delta_P(x)| \le (C+\varepsilon) \frac{x^{\Re \rho_0}}{|\rho_0|}$?

Let $\mathbb{F}$ be a field of characteristic 0, and let $p$ and $q$ be integers with $p \neq q$ and $p,q > 1$. Let $\mathcal{J}_{\mathbb{F}}$ be the Jordan triple system of all $p \times q$ rectangular matrices over $\mathbb{F}$ equipped with the triple product $\{x,y,z\} = x y^T z + z y^T x$. The universal associative envelope of $\mathcal{J}_{\mathbb{F}}$, denoted $\mathcal{U}(\mathcal{J}_{\mathbb{F}})$, is isomorphic to the associative algebra of $N \times N$ matrices over $\mathbb{F}$. What is $N$ in terms of $p$ and $q$?

Let $G$ be a connected graph. The distance from a path $P$ to a vertex $v \in V(G)$ is the minimum distance between $v$ and any vertex in $P$. The eccentricity of a path $P$, denoted by $\mathrm{ecc}_G(P)$, is the maximum distance from $P$ to any vertex in $G$. The path eccentricity of $G$, denoted by $\mathrm{pe}(G)$, is the minimum of $\mathrm{ecc}_G(P)$ over all paths $P$ in $G$. What is the supremum of $\mathrm{pe}(G)$ over all connected bipartite convex graphs $G$?

For a metric space $(X, d)$ and a scale parameter $r \geq 0$, the Vietoris-Rips complex $\mathcal{VR}(X;r)$ is a simplicial complex on vertex set $X$, where a finite set $\sigma \subseteq X$ is a simplex if and only if the diameter of $\sigma$ is at most $r$. Let $\mathbb{I}_n$ denote the $n$-dimensional hypercube graph, equipped with the standard shortest path graph metric. A finite simplicial complex $\Delta$ is $d$-collapsible if it can be reduced to the void complex by repeatedly removing a face of size at most $d$ that is contained in a unique maximal face of $\Delta$. The collapsibility number of $\Delta$ is the minimum integer $d$ such that $\Delta$ is $d$-collapsible. For sufficiently large $n$, what is the collapsibility number of $\mathcal{VR}(\mathbb{I}_n; 3)$?

Consider hypersurfaces $H^n \subset \mathbb{R}^{n+1}$ represented as graphs $\{ u = F(x_1, \dots, x_n) \}$ such that the Hessian matrix $(F_{x_i x_j})$ has a constant rank of $1$. Such a hypersurface is called an affinely homogeneous model if it is homogeneous under the affine group ${\rm Aff}(\mathbb{R}^{n+1})$. An affinely homogeneous model is called a product model if it is affinely equivalent to a product of $\mathbb{R}^m$ (for some $m > 0$) with an affinely homogeneous model in dimension $n-m$. What is the smallest integer dimension $n \ge 2$ in which every affinely homogeneous Hessian rank 1 hypersurface $H^n \subset \mathbb{R}^{n+1}$ is guaranteed to be a product model?

In a $d$-dimensional spatial voting model, a finite set of candidates is located in $\mathbb{R}^d$. Every point in $\mathbb{R}^d$ represents a possible voter ideal point, and each voter ranks the candidates according to their $\ell_1$ distance from the voter's ideal point (with a strictly greater distance corresponding to a strictly lower ranking). Assuming the candidates' positions are chosen to maximize this quantity, what is the maximum possible number of distinct candidates that can be ranked in the unique last position (i.e., at a strictly greater $\ell_1$ distance than all other candidates) across all possible voters in $\mathbb{R}^d$?

Let $\rho(X,Y)$ denote the Pearson correlation coefficient between two non-constant real-valued random variables $X$ and $Y$ with finite non-zero variances. The absolute correlation distance is defined as $d(X,Y) = 1 - |\rho(X,Y)|$. For this distance measure, there exists a constant $K$ such that the $K$-relaxed triangle inequality $d(X,Z) \le K(d(X,Y) + d(Y,Z))$ holds for all such random variables $X, Y,$ and $Z$. What is the minimum possible value of the constant $K$?

Let $I$ be the homogeneous ideal defining the set of triple points of a Böröczky line arrangement of $n$ lines in the projective plane. What is the smallest integer $n$ for which the symbolic cube $I^{(3)}$ is not contained in the ordinary square $I^2$?

Consider the problem of low-rank approximation under the Schatten-$p$ norm: given access to a matrix $A$ through matrix-vector products, an accuracy parameter $\epsilon > 0$, and a target rank $k$, the goal is to find a rank-$k$ matrix $Z$ with orthonormal columns such that $\| A(I - ZZ^\top)\|_{S_p} \leq (1+\epsilon)\min_{U^\top U = I_k} \|A(I - U U^\top)\|_{S_p}$. For any fixed constant $p \geq 1$ and constant target rank $k$, it is known that the optimal matrix-vector query complexity of this problem is $\tilde{\Theta}(1/\epsilon^c)$, where $\tilde{\Theta}$ suppresses poly-logarithmic factors in the matrix dimensions, $k$, and $1/\epsilon$. What is the value of the exponent $c$?

Let $X$ be a simply connected Calabi-Yau threefold with second Betti number $b_2=3$ and torsion-free homology. The cup-product on the second integral cohomology defines a cubic form, which we assume defines a smooth real elliptic curve in $\mathbb{P}^2(\mathbb{R})$ whose Hessian is irreducible. Assuming further that there are no rigid non-movable surfaces $E$ on $X$ with $1 \le E^3 \le 8$, exactly how many connected components must this real elliptic curve have?

Let $R=K[x_1,\ldots, x_n]$ be the polynomial ring in $n$ variables over a field $K$ and let $I \subseteq R$ be a monomial ideal generated by monomials of degree at most $2$. For all integers $k\geq 1$, what is the ideal quotient $(I^{k+1}:I)$ equal to? Express your answer in terms of $I$ and $k$.

Marked ordered trees are defined as ordered trees where each rightmost edge (the edge connecting an internal node to its rightmost child) may optionally be marked, provided that the edge does not lead to a leaf. Let $\mu_n$ be the expected number of deepest nodes in a tree chosen uniformly at random from the set of all marked ordered trees with $n$ nodes. What is $\lim_{n \to \infty} \mu_n$?

Let $M_m$ denote the maximum absolute value of the aperiodic autocorrelation (at non-zero shifts) of the $m$-th Rudin-Shapiro sequence of length $2^m$. It is known that $M_m = \Theta(\lambda^m)$ as $m \to \infty$ for a unique real number $\lambda > 1$. What is the minimal monic polynomial of $\lambda$ over $\mathbb{Q}$?

For a connected graph $G$ of order $n$ with independence number $\alpha$, let $D(G)$ be the distance matrix of $G$ and let $\operatorname{Diag}(Tr)$ be the diagonal matrix of vertex transmissions. The distance Laplacian matrix of $G$ is defined as $D^L(G)=\operatorname{Diag}(Tr)-D(G)$. Let $m_{D^{L} (G)}(I)$ denote the number of eigenvalues of $D^{L}(G)$ which lie in the interval $I$. What is the tight upper bound on $m_{D^{L}(G)}[n,n+\alpha]$ valid for all such graphs $G$, given as a closed-form expression in terms of $n$ and $\alpha$?

Consider the Schnakenberg model on a two-dimensional domain. Suppose we have a highly symmetric configuration of $N$ spikes whose locations are at the vertices of a regular $N$-gon inside a unit disk (a ring of spikes). What is the maximum integer $N$ for which this ring of spikes can be stable, provided that the feed-rate parameter is sufficiently large?

Let $X(K, U)$ be an Oeljeklaus-Toma manifold constructed from a number field $K$ of signature $(s, t)$ with $s \geq 1$ and $t \geq 2$. It is proven that $X(K, U)$ admits no locally conformally K\"ahler metric if $s \geq k t$. Combined with prior results, this condition completely solves the problem of the existence of locally conformally K\"ahler metrics on Oeljeklaus-Toma manifolds, meaning the coefficient $k$ is the exact threshold. What is the value of the integer $k$?

Consider a Schrödinger operator $H = -\Delta + V(x)$ on $\mathbb{R}^4$ with a real potential $V(x)$ such that $\langle x \rangle^{4+\varepsilon} V \in L^1(\mathbb{R}^4) \cap L^4(\mathbb{R}^4)$ for some $\varepsilon > 0$. Suppose that $H$ has resonances at the threshold. Let $W_\pm$ be the wave operators associated with $H$. What is the supremum of $p \ge 1$ such that the low energy parts $W_{\pm, \leq a} = W_\pm \chi_{\leq a}(|D|)$ (for any arbitrary $a>0$) are bounded operators on $L^p(\mathbb{R}^4)$?

Let $q^k n^2$ be an odd perfect number with special prime $q$ (so $q \equiv k \equiv 1 \pmod 4$ and $\gcd(q,n)=1$). Define the greatest common divisors $G = \gcd(\sigma(q^k),\sigma(n^2))$, $H = \gcd(n^2,\sigma(n^2))$, and $I = \gcd(n,\sigma(n^2))$, where $\sigma(x)$ denotes the sum of divisors of $x$. What is the value of the product $G \times H$ expressed in terms of $I$?

Let $h \geq 3$ be an integer. Consider $h$ independent simple, symmetric random walks on the integer lattice $\mathbb{Z}^2$, all starting at the origin, denoted by $\{S_n^{(k)}\}_{n \geq 1}$ for $1 \leq k \leq h$. For $1 \leq i < j \leq h$, let $\mathsf{L}_N^{(i,j)} = \sum_{n=1}^N \mathbf{1}_{\{S_n^{(i)} = S_n^{(j)}\}}$ be the number of collisions between the $i$-th and $j$-th walks up to step $N$. As $N 	o \infty$, the $\binom{h}{2}$-dimensional family of logarithmically rescaled collision local times
$$ \left\{ \frac{\pi}{\log N} \mathsf{L}_N^{(i,j)} \right\}_{1\leq i<j\leq h} $$
converges jointly in distribution to a continuous random vector $\mathbf{X}$. What is the value of the joint probability density function $f_{\mathbf{X}}$ of the limiting random vector evaluated at a point where every coordinate is equal to $x$, for some $x > 0$? Express your answer as a function of $h$ and $x$.

Consider a variant of the Angel game played on an $n$-dimensional infinite grid by the Drunk Angel and a hiding Devil. Before the game starts, the Devil selects a finite number of turns $N$. In each turn, the Drunk Angel of power $c \in \mathbb{N}$ moves uniformly at random from her current point $(x_1, \dots, x_n)$ to any point $(x_1', \dots, x_n')$ satisfying $\max_{1 \leq i \leq n} |x_i - x_i'| \leq c$. Meanwhile, the Devil secretly destroys one vertex per turn. After the Angel's $N$-th move, the Devil reveals a closed $n$-dimensional shape of thickness $c$ made of the destroyed vertices, and wins if the Angel is inside it; otherwise, the Angel wins. For any given power $c \in \mathbb{N}$ and any $\epsilon > 0$, it is proven that when the Devil plays with the Gauss's circle strategy, he can cage the Drunk Angel with probability greater than $1 - \epsilon$ if and only if the dimension $n$ is less than or equal to a specific integer. What is this integer?

A sub-Riemannian manifold is called a geodesic orbit space if all of its normal geodesics are homogeneous (i.e., they are orbits of one-parameter subgroups of isometries). What is the set of all possible values for the nilpotent step of a Carnot group, endowed with a left-invariant sub-Riemannian structure, that is a geodesic orbit space?

Let $p_n$ be the number of polyhedral orientations with $n$ inner vertices and $s_n$ be the number of Schnyder labelings of $(6,4)$-dissections with $n$ inner faces. What is the exact value of the ordered pair of asymptotic growth constants $\left(\lim_{n \to \infty} (p_n)^{1/n}, \lim_{n \to \infty} (s_n)^{1/n}\right)$?

Consider the Erdős-Rényi random graph process on $n$ vertices, where a new uniformly distributed edge is added to the graph in every step. For a fixed integer $d \ge 1$, it has been proven that with high probability (as $n \to \infty$), the graph becomes globally rigid in $\mathbb{R}^d$ at the exact moment its minimum degree reaches a specific threshold. What is this minimum degree threshold as a function of $d$?

Let $n, k, s,$ and $\ell$ be positive integers such that $n = sk + \ell$ with $1 \le \ell \le k$ and $s \ge 4\ell$. For $i=0, \ldots, s$, let $\mathcal{F}_i$ be a non-empty family of $k$-element subsets of $[n] = \{1, 2, \ldots, n\}$. The sequence of families $\mathcal{F}_0, \ldots, \mathcal{F}_s$ is called cross-union if there is no choice of sets $F_0 \in \mathcal{F}_0, \ldots, F_s \in \mathcal{F}_s$ such that $F_0 \cup \dots \cup F_s = [n]$. What is the maximum possible value of the arithmetic mean of their sizes, $\frac{1}{s+1} \sum_{i=0}^s |\mathcal{F}_i|$?

Let $A$ and $B$ be $n \times n$ complex matrices with $n \ge 2$. For a real parameter $q > 0$, define the $q$-deformed commutator as $[A, B]_q := AB - qBA$. Let $\| \cdot \|_F$ denote the Frobenius norm. Suppose that at least one of $A$ or $B$ is a normal matrix. There exists a minimal real-valued function $C(q)$ such that the inequality $\| [ A, B ]_q \|_F^2 \le C(q) \|A\|_F^2 \|B\|_F^2$ holds for all such matrices $A$ and $B$ (of any size $n \ge 2$). What is the function $C(q)$?

What is the number of topological complex vector bundles (up to isomorphism) of rank $n-2$ with vanishing Chern classes over the complex projective space $\mathbb{C}P^n$ for any integer $n > 3$ such that $n \equiv 2 \pmod{24}$?

Consider the construction of a $J$-orthogonal basis for the extended Krylov subspace $\mathcal{K}_{r,s}=\operatorname{range}\{u,Hu, H^2u, \ldots, H^{2r-1}u, H^{-1}u, H^{-2}u, \ldots, H^{-2s}u\}$, where $H \in \mathbb{R}^{2n \times 2n}$ is a Hamiltonian matrix and $r = s+1$ or $r=s$. This basis can be generated using a short recurrence relation. At most how many previously generated basis vectors are involved in this short recurrence?

In composite convex optimization, consider finding an $\epsilon$-approximate minimizer of an objective function whose smooth part is three-times continuously differentiable with Lipschitz continuous third-order derivatives. An accelerated adaptive third-order method computes trial points by inexactly minimizing a model consisting of the nonsmooth part plus a quartic regularization of the third-order Taylor polynomial of the smooth part, using a Bregman gradient method as the inner solver. The total inner iteration complexity of this accelerated method to achieve an $\epsilon$-approximate minimizer is $\mathcal{O}\left(|\log(\epsilon)|\epsilon^{-p}\right)$. What is the value of the exponent $p$?

Consider the equivalence problem of nondegenerate real hypersurfaces $M \subset \mathbb{C}^2$ under the pseudo-group action of holomorphic transformations. Under a suitable genericity condition, the entire algebra of differential invariants for hypersurfaces that are non-umbilic at a point can be generated, through the operators of invariant differentiation, by a single real differential invariant. What is the order of this generating differential invariant?

Let $G$ be a finite group and $K$ a finite field of characteristic $2$. Denote by $t$ the $2$-rank of the commutator factor group $G/G'$ and by $s$ the number of isomorphism classes of self-dual simple $KG$-modules. The Witt group of equivariant quadratic forms $WQ(K,G)$ is isomorphic to an elementary abelian $2$-group of rank $r$. What is $r$ in terms of $s$ and $t$?

Let $M(x)$ be the ordinary generating function of rooted 3-connected bipartite planar maps, where the coefficient of $x^n$ is the number of such maps with $n$ edges. It is a known result that $M(x)$ is an algebraic function over $\mathbb{Q}(x)$. What is the degree of the minimal polynomial of $M(x)$ over $\mathbb{Q}(x)$?

For a self-repelling elastic manifold with domain $[-N,N]^2 \cap \mathbb{Z}^2$ taking values in $\mathbb{R}^1$, Kantor, Kardar, and Nelson conjectured that the effective radius $R_N$ scales with $N$ as $N^\alpha$. What is the exact value of the scaling exponent $\alpha$?

Let $\mathbb{H} = \{z=x+iy \in \mathbb{C} : y>0\}$. For $\alpha \geq 1$ and $z \in \mathbb{H}$, define the theta function $\theta(\alpha; z) = \sum_{(m,n)\in\mathbb{Z}^2} e^{-\alpha\frac{\pi}{y}|mz+n|^2}$. Consider the problem of minimizing the difference $\theta(\alpha; z) - \beta\theta(2\alpha; z)$ over all $z \in \mathbb{H}$, where $\beta \in \mathbb{R}$. There exists a critical value $\beta_c$ (independent of $\alpha$) such that if $\beta \leq \beta_c$, the minimum is attained at $z = \frac{1}{2}+i\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$ (up to equivalence of the associated lattice $\mathbb{Z} \oplus z\mathbb{Z}$), and if $\beta > \beta_c$, no minimizer exists in $\mathbb{H}$. What is the exact value of $\beta_c$?

A tri-linear rational map in dimension three is a rational map $\phi: (\mathbb{P}_\mathbb{C}^1)^3 \dashrightarrow \mathbb{P}_\mathbb{C}^3$ defined by four tri-linear polynomials without a common factor. Let $\mathfrak{Bir}_{(1,1,1)}$ be the set of such maps that are birational, up to composition with an automorphism of $\mathbb{P}_\mathbb{C}^3$. The group action on $\mathfrak{Bir}_{(1,1,1)}$ given by composition with automorphisms of $(\mathbb{P}_\mathbb{C}^1)^3$ defines a finite number of orbits. What is the exact number of orbits defined by this group action?

Consider the Cole-Hopf solution of the (1+1)-dimensional Kardar-Parisi-Zhang (KPZ) equation $\mathcal{H}^f(t,x)$ started with deterministic continuous initial data $f$ satisfying $f(x) \ll 1 + x^2$. Let $\mathcal{H}_t^f := \mathcal{H}^f(t,0)$ denote the KPZ temporal process. It has been established that the temporal increments of $\mathcal{H}_t^f$ are well approximated by the increments of a fractional Brownian motion. What is the exact value of the Hurst parameter of this fractional Brownian motion?

Consider a weak solution $(u, b)$ to the 3D incompressible magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) equations on $\mathbb{R}^3 \times (0,T)$ with velocity field $u$, magnetic field $b$, and pressure $\pi$, where the initial data satisfies $(u_0, b_0) \in L^2(\mathbb{R}^3) \cap L^4(\mathbb{R}^3)$ and $\nabla \cdot u_0 = \nabla \cdot b_0 = 0$. A regularity criterion states that the solution is regular on $(0,T]$ provided that
$$ \frac{\pi}{( e^{-|x|^2}+|u|+|b| )^\theta} \in L^p(0,T;L^{q,\infty }(\mathbb{R}^3)) $$
for a given constant $0 \leq \theta \leq 1$. What is the required value of $\frac{2}{p} + \frac{3}{q}$ in terms of $\theta$ for this criterion to hold?

Consider the 1d Schrödinger operator with a decaying random potential with decaying rate $\alpha > 0$. In the joint scaling limit of the eigenvalues and the measures associated with the corresponding eigenfunctions (based on the formulation by Rifkind-Virág), the behavior of the limiting measure depends on $\alpha$. There exists a critical value $\alpha_c$ such that for $\alpha > \alpha_c$ the limiting measure is the Lebesgue measure, for $\alpha < \alpha_c$ the limiting measure is a delta measure with its atom being uniformly distributed, and for $\alpha = \alpha_c$ the limiting measure has a density with power-law decay and Brownian fluctuation. What is the value of $\alpha_c$?

The Kneser graph $K(v,n)$ is the graph whose vertices are the $n$-element subsets of a $v$-element set, with an edge between two vertices if and only if the corresponding sets are disjoint (where $v > 2n$). A set of vertices $S$ in a graph $G$ is geodetically convex if it contains all vertices on all shortest paths between any two vertices in $S$. The geodetic hull of $S$ is the intersection of all geodetically convex sets containing $S$. The geodetic hull number of a graph $G$ is the minimum size of a subset of vertices whose geodetic hull is the entire vertex set of $G$.

It is known that all Kneser graphs of diameter two have a geodetic hull number of 2, except for exactly three specific Kneser graphs which have a geodetic hull number of 3. Let $V_1, V_2,$ and $V_3$ denote the vertex sets of these three exceptional Kneser graphs. What is the ordered tuple $(|V_1|, |V_2|, |V_3|)$ of the number of vertices of these three graphs, sorted such that $|V_1| < |V_2| < |V_3|$?

Let $P_N(\alpha) := \prod_{n=1}^{N}\lvert 2\sin(\pi n \alpha)\rvert$ denote the Sudler product. Let $\psi: (0,\infty) \to (0,\infty)$ be a non-decreasing function such that $\sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\psi(k)} = \infty$ and $\liminf_{k \to \infty} \frac{\psi(k)}{k \log k} \geq C$ for a sufficiently large constant $C > 0$. For Lebesgue-almost every irrational $\alpha$, what is the exact value of the upper density of the set $\{N \in \mathbb{N}: \log P_N(\alpha) \geq \psi(\log N)\}$?

Consider the parabolic obstacle problem for the fractional Laplacian $(-\Delta)^s$. Under the assumption that the free boundary is already $C^1$, it has been established that the free boundary is actually $C^{2,\alpha}$. This higher regularity upgrade holds in the regime $s > s_0$. What is the exact value of the threshold $s_0$?

Let $Y_k$ denote the $K3$ surface belonging to the Apéry-Fermi pencil with parameter $k$. Consider an elliptic fibration of the singular $K3$ surface $Y_2$ that possesses a $3$-torsion section, and let $\tau$ be the automorphism of order $3$ on $Y_2$ defined by this section. What is the value of the parameter $k$ such that the quotient surface $Y_2/\tau$ is isomorphic to $Y_k$?

Let $M=\mathbb{R}\times \Sigma$ be a four-dimensional globally hyperbolic spacetime in the ultrastatic case with $\Sigma$ compact, and with metric regularity $C^\tau$. Let $K_G$ be the causal propagator of the Klein-Gordon operator. Let $C \subset T^*(M\times M)$ consist of the points $(\tilde{x},\tilde{\xi},\tilde{y},\tilde{\eta})$ such that $\tilde{\xi}$ and $\tilde{\eta}$ are cotangent to a null geodesic $\gamma$ at $\tilde{x}$ and $\tilde{y}$ respectively, and are parallel transports of each other along $\gamma$. For $\tau>3$ and $0<\varepsilon<\tau-3$, the Sobolev wavefront set of $K_G$ is proven to satisfy the exact equality $WF'^{f(\tau)-\varepsilon}(K_G) = C$ for a specific function $f(\tau)$. What is $f(\tau)$?

Let $P_q(n, m)$ be the probability that a uniformly random monic polynomial of degree $n$ over a finite field $\mathbb{F}_q$ is $m$-smooth (meaning all of its irreducible factors have degree at most $m$), and let $P_{\text{perm}}(n, m)$ be the probability that a uniformly random permutation on $n$ elements is $m$-smooth (meaning all of its disjoint cycles have length at most $m$). It is a known result that $P_q(n, m)$ is asymptotic to $P_{\text{perm}}(n, m)$ uniformly for $m \ge (c+\varepsilon)\log_q n$ as $q^n \to \infty$. What is the value of the sharp constant $c$?

Consider Boolean functions on the slice $\binom{[n]}{k}$, which is the set of all vectors in $\{0,1\}^n$ of Hamming weight $k$. A function on the slice is said to be of degree $d$ if it can be represented as the restriction of a polynomial of degree $d$ in $n$ variables to the slice. What is the minimum value $k_{\min}$ (as a function of $d$) such that for all $n \geq 2k$, any Boolean degree $d$ function on the slice $\binom{[n]}{k}$ is guaranteed to be a junta whenever $k \geq k_{\min}$? Assume the bound is sharp, meaning for $k < k_{\min}$, there exist $n \geq 2k$ and degree $d$ functions on $\binom{[n]}{k}$ that are not juntas.

Let $G=(V,E)$ be a simple, undirected graph. For an integer $k \ge 2$, a subset $S \subseteq V$ is defined to be $l^k$-convex if for any pair of vertices $x,y \in S$, every induced path of length at most $k$ connecting $x$ and $y$ has all its vertices in $S$. The $l^k$-convexity of $G$ is the collection of all $l^k$-convex subsets of $V$. A graph $G$ is called an $l^k$-convex geometry if its $l^k$-convexity forms a convex geometry (i.e., satisfies the anti-exchange property). It is a proven result that a graph $G$ is an $l^2$-convex geometry if and only if $G$ is a chordal graph that does not contain $P_n$ (an induced path on $n$ vertices) as an induced subgraph. What is the value of $n$?

Consider the weakly self-avoiding walk with a sufficiently small parameter $\beta > 0$ on a $d$-dimensional discrete torus of volume $V$, where $d>4$. The scaling limit of this walk is proven to be Brownian motion on the continuum torus provided that the length of the walk to be rescaled is $o(V^\alpha)$. If this exponent is sharp, what is the value of $\alpha$?

Consider the Dirichlet eigenvalues of the Laplacian among a Poissonian cloud of hard spherical obstacles of fixed radius in a large box in $\mathbb{R}^d$ ($d \ge 2$) of side-length $2l$ centered at the origin. The lowest eigenvalue is known to be typically of order $(\log l)^{-2/d}$. It is proven that with probability arbitrarily close to $1$ as $l \to \infty$, the spectral gap between the first two eigenvalues is bounded below by $\sigma(\log l)^{-E}$, where $\sigma>0$ is a small constant depending on the desired probability, and this scale is expected to capture the correct size of the gap. What is the exact value of the exponent $E$ as a function of $d$?

Consider critical site percolation on the two-dimensional triangular lattice. Let $P_n(x_1,\dots,x_n)$ denote the scaling limit of the probability that $n$ distinct points $x_1, \dots, x_n \in \mathbb{R}^2$ belong to the same open cluster. It is established that $P_n$ transforms covariantly under global scaling such that $P_n(s x_1, \dots, s x_n) = s^{-\Delta_n} P_n(x_1, \dots, x_n)$ for any scale factor $s>0$. What is the exact value of the scaling exponent $\Delta_n$ as a function of $n$?

Let $A= rac{1}{\sqrt{n}}(a_{ij})_{1 \leq i,j \leq n} \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}$ be a symmetric matrix with $(a_{ij})_{1 \leq i \leq j \leq n}$ i.i.d. random variables with a symmetric distribution such that $\mathbb{E}[a_{11}^2]=1$. When $\lim_{x \to \infty}{x^4\mathbb{P}(|a_{11}|>x)}=0$, the largest eigenvalue $\lambda_1(A)$ converges almost surely to 2. In the edge case where $\lim_{x \to \infty}{x^4\mathbb{P}(|a_{11}|>x)}=c \in (0,\infty)$, the limiting distribution of $\lambda_1(A)$ is given by $f(X)$, where $X$ follows a Fréchet distribution and $f: (0, \infty) \to \mathbb{R}$ is a continuous function. If $f(x) = 2$ for $0 < x < 1$, what is the closed-form expression for $f(x)$ when $x \geq 1$?

Consider a nontrivial $2$-$(k^2, k, \lambda)$ design $\mathcal{D}$ with $\lambda \mid k$, admitting a flag-transitive automorphism group $G$. By classification, $G$ is either an affine group, an almost simple classical group, or an exceptional case where $G$ is the smallest Ree group. In this exceptional case where $G$ is the smallest Ree group, what is the exact value of the block size $k$?

Let $\mathcal{D}=(\mathcal{P},\mathcal{B})$ be a symmetric $2$-$(v,k,\lambda)$ design admitting a flag-transitive, point-imprimitive automorphism group $G$ that leaves invariant a non-trivial partition $\Sigma$ of $\mathcal{P}$. By a theorem of Praeger and Zhou, there is a constant $k_0$ such that for each $B \in \mathcal{B}$ and $\Delta \in \Sigma$, $|B \cap \Delta|$ is either $0$ or $k_0$. Suppose that $k > \lambda(\lambda-3)/2$ and $k_0 \geq 3$. What is the set of all possible parameter triples $(v,k,\lambda)$ for such a design?

Let $\mathcal{G}_n$ be the set of all connected nonregular graphs of order $n$ with maximum degree $3$. Let $G_n \in \mathcal{G}_n$ be a graph that attains the maximum spectral radius among all graphs in $\mathcal{G}_n$, and let $\lambda_1(G_n)$ denote its spectral radius. What is the exact value of the limit $\lim_{n\to\infty} \frac{n^2(3-\lambda_1(G_n))}{2}$?

Let $Av_n(S)$ denote the set of all permutations of length $n$ that avoid all of the patterns in the set $S$. Let $F_k$ denote the $k$-th Fibonacci number, defined by the recurrence $F_k = F_{k-1} + F_{k-2}$ with the initial conditions $F_0 = 1$ and $F_1 = 1$. What is the cardinality $|Av_n(\{231, 312, 1432\})|$ for an integer $n \ge 1$, expressed in terms of the sequence $F$?

Fix an odd prime number $p$. Consider the topological analogues of Iwasawa invariants associated to $\mathbb{Z}_p$-covers of the $3$-sphere branched along a link, determined by an admissible integral vector $z = (z_1, z_2)$ (i.e., $z_1z_2 \neq 0$ and $\gcd(z_1, z_2) = 1$). What is the unconditionally proven density of $2$-bridge links with two components in Schubert normal form $L_{a/b}$ ($b$ even), ordered by the height function $\text{ht}(L_{a/b}) := \max\{a^2, b\}$, for which the topological Iwasawa $\mu$-invariant vanishes and the $\lambda$-invariant is equal to $1$?

Let $q \neq 2$ be a prime power and $\mathbb{F}_q$ be the finite field of order $q$. What is the minimal bi-degree of an irreducible filling curve (an irreducible curve containing all $\mathbb{F}_q$-rational points) for $\mathbb{P}^1\times \mathbb{P}^1$ over $\mathbb{F}_q$?

Let $C_6 \subset \mathbb{P}^5$ be an elliptic normal curve of degree 6 over an algebraically closed field of characteristic 0. Let $C_{pq} \subset \mathbb{P}^3$ be the image of $C_6$ under the projection from a general line in $\mathbb{P}^5$. The homogeneous ideal $I(C_{pq})$ of the projected curve $C_{pq}$ is minimally generated by $m$ homogeneous polynomials of degree 3 and $n$ homogeneous polynomials of degree 4. What is the ordered pair $(m, n)$?

The skew Brownian permuton is a family of random probability measures on the unit square that describes the scaling limit of various random constrained permutations. In its description via Liouville quantum gravity coupled with Schramm-Loewner evolution (SLE), it is associated with a pair of SLE curves having an imaginary geometry angle $\theta$. What is the expected proportion of inversions in a skew Brownian permuton, expressed as a function of $\theta$?

Let $v$ denote the number of vertices of a 2-connected graph $G$, and let $\delta(G)$ denote its minimum degree. It is a theorem that if $\delta(G) \ge f(v)$, then $G$ is guaranteed to contain a cycle $W$ such that the set of vertices $V(G) \setminus V(W)$ is an independent set. This result is best possible in the sense that the statement becomes false if the function $f(v)$ is replaced by any strictly smaller number for a given $v$. What is the expression for $f(v)$?

For integers $k>t>0$ and $n>2k-t$, the generalized Kneser graph $K(n,k,t)$ is the graph whose vertices are the $k$-subsets of $\{1,\dots,n\}$, with two vertices adjacent if and only if they share less than $t$ elements. For each integer $c \ge 1$, there exists a constant $K(c) \ge 2c$ such that if $k \ge K(c)$ and $t=k-c$, then the treewidth $tw(K(n,k,t))$ equals a specific closed-form expression in terms of $n, k,$ and $t$ if and only if $n \ge (t+1)(k+1-t)$. What is this expression for $tw(K(n,k,t))$ when $n \ge (t+1)(k+1-t)$?

Let $n \in \mathbb{N}$ and let $K$ be a field with a henselian discrete valuation of rank $n$ with a hereditarily euclidean residue field. Let $F/K$ be the function field of a hyperelliptic curve of genus $g$. What is the maximum possible order of the multiplicative group of nonzero sums of squares modulo nonzero sums of two squares in $F$ over all such fields $F$?

Let $P_+f$ and $P_-f$ denote the analytic and co-analytic projections of a complex-valued function $f \in L^p(\mathbb{T})$. Consider inequalities of the form:
$$ \left\|\big(|P_+f|^s+|P_-f|^s\big)^{\frac{1}{s}}\right\|_{L^p(\mathbb{T})}\leq A_{p,s} \|f\|_{L^p(\mathbb{T})} $$
For the range $1 < p \leq \frac{4}{3}$, this inequality (with the corresponding best constant) is established for all $s>0$ up to a sharp cutoff value $s_{\max}(p)$. What is the exact value of this sharp cutoff $s_{\max}(p)$ as a function of $p$?

Let $X$ be a non-empty set, $\mathbb{F}$ a subfield of $\mathbb{C}$, and let $A \subseteq \mathbb{F}^X$ be a unital function algebra that contains all bounded functions in $\mathbb{F}^X$. What is the smallest positive integer $k$ such that every element of $A$ is guaranteed to be expressible as a sum of $k$ units in $A$?

Let $W_{t}$ be a standard Brownian motion in the plane $\mathbb{R}^2$ started at the origin and let $\theta$ be the first exit time of the unit disk $D_{1}$. For $x \in \mathbb{R}^2$ and $\epsilon > 0$, let 
$$ \mu_{\theta} ( x,\epsilon) =\frac{1}{\pi\epsilon^{ 2} }\int_{0}^{\theta}\mathbf{1}_{B( x,\epsilon)}( W_{t})\,dt, $$
where $B(x,\epsilon)$ is the open disk of radius $\epsilon$ centered at $x$. Set $\mu^{\ast}_{\theta} (\epsilon)=\sup_{x\in D_{1}}\mu_{\theta} ( x,\epsilon)$. It has been proven that there exist unique constants $a$ and $b$ such that the family of random variables
$$ \sqrt{\mu^{\ast}_\theta (\epsilon)}- a \log(\epsilon^{-1}) - b \log\log(\epsilon^{-1}) $$
is tight as $\epsilon \to 0$. What is the ordered pair $(a,b)$?

Let $F_n$ be a finitely generated free group of rank $n \ge 2$. Consider the geometric realization of the partially ordered set of proper free factors in $F_n$. This space is homotopy equivalent to a wedge of spheres. What is the dimension of these spheres as a function of $n$?

Let $n \ge 1$ be an integer. For any non-empty Borel set $A \subseteq \mathbb R^n$, let $\mathcal{B}_A$ denote the collection of all Borel subsets $B \subseteq \mathbb R^n$ such that their Minkowski sum satisfies $A+B = \mathbb R^n$. Let $p = \inf_{B \in \mathcal{B}_A} \dim_P(B)$, where $\dim_P(B)$ denotes the packing dimension of $B$. What is the Hausdorff dimension of $A$ equal to in terms of $n$ and $p$?

A proper conflict-free $c$-coloring of a graph is a proper $c$-coloring such that each non-isolated vertex has a color appearing exactly once on its neighborhood. Let $mad(G)$ denote the maximum average degree of a graph $G$. What is the exact threshold value $M(c)$ such that for all integers $c \geq 5$, any graph $G$ with $mad(G) \leq M(c)$ is guaranteed to have a proper conflict-free $c$-coloring, provided that $G$ does not contain a $1$-subdivision of the complete graph on $c+1$ vertices?

Let $S$ denote the set of positive integers $m$ such that $m!$ cannot be written as a sum of three squares. The counting function of this set satisfies $|S \cap [1, n]| = c n + \mathcal{O}(\sqrt{n})$ for some constant $c$. What is the exact value of $c$?

Brandão, Harrow, and Horodecki proved that random quantum circuits on $n$ qubits in a brickwork architecture are approximate unitary $t$-designs by lower bounding the spectral gap of the moment operators for local random quantum circuits by $\Omega(n^{-1}t^{-9.5})$. A subsequent work improved this spectral gap lower bound using Gao's quantum union bound and the properties of the Clifford group to $\Omega(n^{-1}t^{-\alpha-o(1)})$, where the $o(1)$ term goes to $0$ as $t\to\infty$. What is the value of the constant $\alpha$?

In Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory (ZF), the statement that the cofinality of $\omega_1$ is $\omega_1$ ($cf(\omega_1) = \omega_1$) is equivalent to the partition relation $\omega_1 \to (\omega_1, \alpha)^2$. Given that $\alpha$ is the least ordinal such that this partition relation is not provable in ZF alone, what is the exact value of the ordinal $\alpha$?

Consider the higher-order parabolic semilinear equation
\[ u_t+(-\Delta)^{d}u=|x|^\alpha|u|^{p}+t^\sigma \mathbf{w}(x) \quad\mbox{for } (x,t)\in\mathbb{R}^{N}\times(0,\infty), \]
where $d\in (0,1)\cup \mathbb{N}$, $p>1$, $N>2d$, $\alpha$ satisfies either $-\alpha\in(0,\min(2d,N))$ or $\alpha\geq 0$, and $\mathbf{w}$ is a suitable given function such that $\int_{\mathbb{R}^N}\mathbf{w}(x)\,dx>0$. For $\sigma \in (-1,0)$, the Fujita critical exponent $p_F(\sigma)$ separates the regimes of finite-time blow-up for all solutions ($1 < p < p_F(\sigma)$) and the existence of unique global-in-time solutions for sufficiently small initial data ($p \geq p_F(\sigma)$). What is the exact expression for the Fujita critical exponent $p_F(\sigma)$ in terms of $N, d, \alpha,$ and $\sigma$?

Let $\{n_k\}$ be a lacunary sequence of integers satisfying $n_{k+1}/n_k > \lambda \ge 3$. Define a sequence $\{m_k\}$ consisting of all possible $l$-wise sums of the form $\pm n_{k_1} \pm n_{k_2} \pm \ldots \pm n_{k_l}$, where $k_1 > k_2 > \ldots > k_l$, for a fixed integer $l \ge 2$. Let $S = \sum_k c_k e^{i m_k x}$ be a finite sum. It is known that there exists a constant $C_{l,\lambda,p}$ such that the Khintchine-type inequality $\|S\|_p \le C_{l,\lambda,p} \|S\|_2$ holds for $p > 2$. As $p \to \infty$, the optimal constant $C_{l,\lambda,p}$ has a sharp growth rate of $\Theta(p^\alpha)$. What is the exact value of the exponent $\alpha$ as a function of $l$?

Consider the original weak Galerkin mixed finite element method (WG-MFEM) for solving second-order elliptic equations with Neumann boundary conditions on 2D domains with curved boundaries. When the curved physical domain is approximated by a polygonal domain such that the maximum boundary edge length $s$ satisfies $s = \mathcal{O}(h)$, the discrepancy causes a loss of accuracy. Specifically, due to the difference between the outward normal vectors on the polygonal and curved domains, a rigorous error analysis reveals that the original WG-MFEM (without boundary correction techniques) exhibits a convergence rate of $\mathcal{O}(h^\beta)$ with respect to the mesh size $h$, for all polynomial degrees $\alpha \ge 1$. What is the value of the exponent $\beta$?

Let $R$ be a real closed field. Let $d \ge 1$ be an integer. What is the sharp upper bound (which is independent of $n$) on the number of semi-algebraically connected components of a real hypersurface in $R^n$ defined by a multi-affine polynomial of degree $d$?

In the Directed Landscape $\mathcal{L}$, consider the 2D weight difference profile defined as $\mathcal{D}(x, t) = \mathcal{L}(-1, 0; x, t) - \mathcal{L}(1, 0; x, t)$ for $(x,t) \in \mathbb{R} \times \mathbb{R}_+$. What is the almost sure Hausdorff dimension of the set of points in $\mathbb{R} \times \mathbb{R}_+$ where $\mathcal{D}$ is not locally constant (i.e., not constant in any neighborhood)?

Let $K = \mathbb{Q}(\alpha)$ be a pure number field generated by a root $\alpha$ of the monic irreducible polynomial $F(x) = x^{2^u \cdot 3^v \cdot 5^t}-m$, where $m 
eq \pm 1$ is a square-free integer and $u, v, t$ are positive integers. It has been proven that $K$ is monogenic if $m$ satisfies the congruences $m 
ot\equiv a \pmod 4$, $m 
ot\equiv \pm b \pmod 9$, and $m 
ot\equiv \pm c \pmod{25}$ and $m 
ot\equiv \pm d \pmod{25}$, where $a, b, c, d$ are integers such that $0 \le a \le 3$, $0 \le b \le 4$, and $0 \le c < d \le 12$. What is the ordered tuple $(a, b, c, d)$?

Let $G$ be the graph of a connected triangulated $(d-1)$-manifold, for a fixed integer $d\geq 4$. Assume $G$ is not a complete graph. It is a proven theorem that $G$ is generically globally rigid in $\mathbb R^d$ if and only if $G$ is $k$-connected. What is the value of $k$ as a function of $d$?

Consider the generalized Benjamin-Ono equation $\partial_t u + \partial_x(-|D|u + |u|^{p-1}u) = 0$ with $L^2$-supercritical power $p>3$ or $L^2$-subcritical power $2<p<3$. Strongly interacting multi-solitary wave solutions exist of the form $\sum_{i=1}^n \sigma_i Q(\cdot-t-x_i(t))$, where $n\geq 2$, $\sigma_i = (-1)^{i-1}$ if $2<p<3$, and $\sigma_i = 1$ if $p>3$. As $t\rightarrow +\infty$, the distances between adjacent solitary waves satisfy $x_{i}(t)-x_{i+1}(t)\sim c_i t^\beta$ for some universal positive constants $c_i$. What is the exact value of the exponent $\beta$?

Let $\Gamma$ be the fundamental group of the Brieskorn homology sphere $\Sigma(2,3,7)$, given by the presentation $\Gamma = \langle a, b, c \mid a^2 = b^3 = c^7 = abc \rangle$. The group $\Gamma$ acts on its space of left-orders by conjugation. What is the exact number of minimal components (minimal closed invariant subsets) for this action?

Consider the problem of testing whether a symmetric $d 	imes d$ input matrix $A$ is symmetric positive semidefinite (PSD) or is $\epsilon$-far from the PSD cone, where being $\epsilon$-far means $\lambda_{\min}(A) \leq - \epsilon\|A\|_p$, with $\|A\|_p$ denoting the Schatten-$p$ norm of $A$. A one-sided tester correctly classifies any PSD input but may fail on a non-PSD input with a tiny failure probability. In the matrix-vector query model, it has been shown that for any $p \geq 1$, the optimal number of queries required by an adaptive one-sided tester is tightly bounded, up to logarithmic factors, by $\widetilde{\Theta}(1/\epsilon^c)$. What is the exact expression for the exponent $c$ as a function of $p$?

Let $n \ge 2$ be an integer. Consider the equation $x^{2^{3n}+2^{2n}+2^{n}-1}+(x+1)^{2^{3n}+2^{2n}+2^{n}-1}=b$ for $x \in GF(2^{4n})$. For how many values of $b \in GF(2^{4n})$ does this equation have exactly $2^{2n}-2^n$ solutions?

Let $T = (V, E)$ be a complete full $t$-ary tree for an odd integer $t \ge 3$, which is a rooted tree in which all non-leaf nodes have exactly $t$ children and every leaf is at the same depth. Let $l$ denote the number of leaves of $T$. A bijection $f : E \rightarrow \{1, 2,\dots, |E|\}$ is a local antimagic labeling if for any adjacent vertices $u$ and $v$, their vertex sums $w(u) = \sum_{e \in E(u)} f(e)$ and $w(v) = \sum_{e \in E(v)} f(e)$ are distinct, where $E(x)$ denotes the set of edges incident to a vertex $x$. The local antimagic chromatic number $\chi_{la}(T)$ is defined as the minimum number of distinct vertex sums (i.e., $|\{w(v) : v \in V\}|$) taken over all local antimagic labelings of $T$. What is the exact value of $\chi_{la}(T)$ in terms of $l$?

Consider tabular offline reinforcement learning for a finite-horizon Markov decision process (MDP) with $S$ states, horizon $H$, and a single-policy clipped concentrability coefficient denoted by $C$. It has been established that a model-based (or 'plug-in') approach achieves the minimax-optimal sample complexity without burn-in cost. What is this minimax-optimal sample complexity to achieve $\varepsilon$-accuracy? Provide the exact expression representing the leading polynomial terms (ignoring constant and logarithmic factors) in terms of $S$, $H$, $C$, and $\varepsilon$. Omit any asymptotic notation (such as $\mathcal{O}$ or $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}$) and assume a leading coefficient of 1.

Let $d$ be an integer greater than $1$. A recent theorem establishes that if $t$ is a fixed real number satisfying $\frac{1}{d} < t < f(d)$, then for any integer $n_0$ chosen sufficiently large depending upon $t$, the $d$-dimensional cubes of sidelength $n^{-t}$ for $n \geq n_0$ can perfectly pack a $d$-dimensional cube of volume $\sum_{n=n_0}^\infty \frac{1}{n^{dt}}$. What is the expression for the upper bound $f(d)$ given in this theorem?

Let $u_\epsilon : M^n \to \mathbb{C}$ be a family of critical points for the complex Ginzburg-Landau energies
$$E_\epsilon(u) = \int_M \left( \frac{|du|^2}{2} + \frac{(1-|u|^2)^2}{4\epsilon^2} \right)$$
on a manifold $M$ of dimension $n \ge 3$, with $E_\epsilon(u_\epsilon) = O(|\log \epsilon|)$. As $\epsilon \to 0$, the concentrated portion of the normalized energy measures limits to a stationary, rectifiable $(n-2)$-varifold $V$. It is proven that the $(n-2)$-density $\Theta_{n-2}(|V|, x)$ of the limit varifold takes values in a sharp set $S \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ at $|V|$-a.e. $x \in M$, where "sharp" means for any $\theta \in S$, there exists a family of critical points in $B_1^n(0)$ whose concentration varifold is an $(n-2)$-plane with density $\theta$. It is a known phenomenon that the density cannot take values in a certain gap strictly above 1. What is the supremum of $c > 1$ such that $S \cap (1, c) = \emptyset$?

Consider curves of genus $5$ defined over the finite field $\mathbb{F}_3$. What is the maximum possible number of $\mathbb{F}_9$-rational points on such a curve, provided that the curve is either hyperelliptic or trigonal?

Let $G=(U, V, E)$ be a simple bipartite graph with parts $U=\{u_1, u_2, \dots, u_n\}$ and $V=\{v_1, v_2, \dots, v_n\}$. For a given integer $k$ satisfying $n \ge k > \frac{n}{2}$, what is the maximum possible number of pairs $(i,j) \in \{1, \dots, n\} \times \{1, \dots, n\}$ such that $|\text{deg}(u_i)-\text{deg}(v_j)| \ge k$? Express your answer as a closed-form function of $n$ and $k$.

Let $P_n$ and $K_n$ denote the induced path and complete graph on $n$ vertices, respectively. The kite is the graph obtained from a $P_4$ by adding a vertex and making it adjacent to all vertices in the $P_4$ except one vertex with degree 1. What is the maximum possible chromatic number of a finite simple graph that contains no induced subgraph isomorphic to a $P_5$, a kite, or a $K_7$?

The reciprocal distance matrix $RD(G)$ of a connected graph $G$ is the square matrix indexed by the vertices of $G$, where the $(u,v)$-entry is $1/d(u,v)$ if $u \neq v$, and $0$ if $u = v$, with $d(u,v)$ being the shortest path distance between $u$ and $v$ in $G$. The reciprocal distance energy of $G$ is the sum of the absolute values of the eigenvalues of $RD(G)$. Consider the complete bipartite graph $K_{m,m}$ for an integer $m \ge 2$. Because $K_{m,m}$ is edge-transitive, the graph $K_{m,m} - e$ obtained by deleting a single edge $e$ is unique up to isomorphism. What is the maximum integer $m \ge 2$ such that the reciprocal distance energy of $K_{m,m} - e$ is strictly less than the reciprocal distance energy of $K_{m,m}$?

Let $G$ be a bridgeless cubic graph. A $1^+$-factor of $G$ is defined as a spanning subgraph where every vertex has degree at least 1. What is the smallest integer $k$ such that for every bridgeless cubic graph $G$, every $1^+$-factor $F$ of $G$, and every edge $e$ of $G$, there is guaranteed to exist a perfect matching $M$ of $G$ containing $e$ such that the graph $G \setminus (F \cup M)$ is $k$-colorable?

Let $(M, c, \nabla)$ be a non-closed recurrent Lorentzian Weyl manifold that is also an Einstein-Weyl manifold. Let $d$ be the dimension of $M$ and $h$ be the dimension of the holonomy algebra of the Weyl connection $\nabla$. According to the complete classification of such manifolds, what is the exact value of $d + h$?

For a graph $G$, the central graph $C(G)$ is constructed from $G$ by subdividing each edge of $G$ with exactly one vertex and adding an edge between every pair of non-adjacent vertices in $G$. Let $\gamma(H)$ denote the domination number of a graph $H$, and let $	au(G)$ be the minimum cardinality of a vertex cover of $G$. It has been shown that for any graph $G$ with at least three vertices and at least one edge, the difference $\gamma(C(G)) - 	au(G)$ can only take on exactly two integer values across all such graphs. What is the set of these two possible values?

Given a family $\mathcal{G}$ of finite graphs spanning a common finite vertex set $V$, a cooperative coloring of $\mathcal{G}$ is a collection of one independent set from each graph of $\mathcal{G}$ such that the union of these independent sets equals $V$. Let $m(d)$ be the minimum integer $k$ such that every family of at least $k$ finite star forests, each of maximum degree at most $d$, on a common finite vertex set admits a cooperative coloring. As $d \to \infty$, it is proven that $m(d) = (1+o(1)) f(d)$, where $f(d)$ is a simple expression without constant factors. What is $f(d)$? Express your answer using $d$ and the $\log$ function.

Consider the energy critical four-dimensional semi-linear heat equation $\partial_{t}v-\Delta v-v^{3}=0$ for $(t,x)\in \mathbb{R}\times \mathbb{R}^4$. There exists a sequence of type II blow-up solutions at finite time $T>0$ whose $L^\infty(\mathbb{R}^4)$ norm scales asymptotically as $t \nearrow T$ according to $\|v(t)\|_{L^\infty(\mathbb{R}^4)} \approx \frac{|\log(T-t)|^{\alpha_L}}{(T-t)^L}$ for integers $L \geq 1$. What is the exact value of the exponent $\alpha_L$ as a function of $L$?

Consider the Cauchy problem for the complex valued semi-linear heat equation in spatial dimension $d$:
$$ \partial_t u - \Delta u - u^m =0, \quad u (0,x) = u_0(x), $$
where $m\geq 2$ is an integer. Let $E^s_\sigma$ be the super-critical function space equipped with the norm
$$ \|f\|_{E^s_\sigma} = \|\langle \xi\rangle^\sigma 2^{s|\xi|}\hat{f}(\xi)\|_{L^2}. $$
For $s<0$, global existence and uniqueness of solutions are established for initial data in $E^s_\sigma$ whose Fourier transforms are supported in the first octant away from the origin (without requiring smallness conditions on the initial data) provided that $\sigma$ is greater than or equal to a critical threshold $\sigma_c(d, m)$. What is the explicit expression for $\sigma_c(d, m)$?

Let $G=(V,E)$ be a finite connected graph with $n=|V| \ge 2$ vertices. For each vertex $u \in V$, let $s(u) = \sum_{v \in V} d(u,v)$ be the sum of the shortest-path distances from $u$ to all other vertices in $G$. Define the mean distance $\bar{l} = \frac{1}{n(n-1)} \sum_{u \in V} s(u)$ and the normalized closeness centrality $\bar{\mathsf{C}}_C = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{u \in V} \frac{n-1}{s(u)}$. What is the supremum of the product $\bar{l} \bar{\mathsf{C}}_C$ over all finite connected graphs?

Let $\{X_t\}_{t\geq0}$ be a $2$-dimensional critical super-Brownian motion with binary branching mechanism $\psi(u)=u^2$ started from a Poisson random measure whose intensity is the Lebesgue measure on $\mathbb{R}^2$. Denote by $R_t:=\sup\{u>0: X_t(\{x\in\mathbb{R}^2:|x|< u\})=0\}$ the radius of the largest empty ball centered at the origin of $X_t$. It is established that for $r>0$,
$$ \lim_{t\to\infty}\mathbb{P}\left(\frac{R_t}{t^{\alpha}}\geq r\right)=e^{-A(r)}, $$
where $\alpha$ is a constant and $A(r)$ is a function that satisfies $\lim_{r\to\infty}\frac{A(r)}{r^{\beta}}=C$ for some constant $C\in(0,\infty)$. What is the ordered pair $(\alpha, \beta)$?

A holey Schröder design of type $h_1^{n_1} \cdots h_k^{n_k}$, denoted HSD$(h_1^{n_1} \cdots h_k^{n_k})$, is equivalent to a frame idempotent Schröder quasigroup of order $\sum n_i h_i$ with disjoint and spanning missing subquasigroups (holes) of orders $h_i$. It has been proven that for $0 \le u \le n$, an HSD$(3^n u^1)$ exists if and only if $n(n + 2u - 1) \equiv 0 \pmod 4$ and $n \ge 4$, with the exception of exactly two values of $n$ for which the existence remains unresolved (possible exceptions). What is the sum of these two exceptional values of $n$?

Let $n \ge 2$ be an integer. Consider the set of all bounded ratios (Laurent monomials of minors) of a general $n \times n$ totally positive matrix, i.e., those Laurent monomials of minors that are bounded on the set of all $n \times n$ totally positive matrices. The exponent vectors of these bounded ratios naturally form a polyhedral cone. What is the dimension of this polyhedral cone as a function of $n$?

A well-known conjecture by Verstraëte states that there exists an absolute constant $c>0$ such that every graph of average degree at least $c d^k$ contains a pair of disjoint isomorphic subdivisions of the complete graph $K_d$. What is the integer value of the exponent $k$ in this conjecture?

In the stability and convergence analysis of the third-order variable-step backward differentiation formula (BDF3) for linear diffusion equations, a discrete gradient structure and a discrete energy dissipation law are established. This structure is built under the condition that the adjacent time-step ratios are strictly less than a specific upper bound. What is this numerical bound, as stated to four decimal places?

Consider the parabolic equation $h_t+h_{xxxx}+\partial_{xx}(|h_x|^\alpha)=f$ modeling amorphous molecular beam epitaxy. A partial regularity theorem establishes the precise relationship between the parameter $\alpha$ and the parabolic Hausdorff dimension of the possible singular point set $\mathcal{S}$ of suitable weak solutions. Specifically, it is proven that for $f$ in the Morrey space $\mathcal{M}^{m, \frac{\alpha+1}{\alpha}}$ with $m \geq \frac{\alpha+1}{\alpha}$ and $5/3 \leq \alpha < 7/3$, the $d$-dimensional parabolic Hausdorff measure of $\mathcal{S}$ is zero. What is the precise value of $d$ as a function of $\alpha$ established by this result?

Let $\mathcal{C}$ be the family of all finite, simple, connected cactus graphs with at least one edge. The Szeged index $Sz(G)$ and the Wiener index $W(G)$ are well-known topological indices for graphs. What is the exact value of the supremum of the ratio $\frac{Sz(G)}{W(G)}$ over all graphs $G \in \mathcal{C}$?

Let $C$ be a curve of sufficiently high genus $g$ that embeds in a K3 surface $X$ as a generator of $\mathrm{Pic}(X)$. Consider the moduli space $M_C(r; K_C)$ of stable rank $r$ vector bundles on $C$ with canonical determinant, and let $h$ be the maximum number of linearly independent global sections of these bundles. Under these conditions, the Brill-Noether locus $BN_C \subset M_C(r; K_C)$ of bundles with $h$ global sections is a smooth projective Hyperkähler manifold. What is the dimension of $BN_C$ as a function of $g$ and $r$?

For any Legendrian spatial embedding of a Petersen graph, it is known that the sum of the Thurston-Bennequin numbers of all of its cycles is equal to a constant integer $c$ times the sum of the Thurston-Bennequin numbers of all of its $5$-cycles. What is the value of $c$?

A subset $S\subset\{1,2,\dots,n\}$ is called a Sidon set if all the sums $a+b$ for $a,b\in S$ with $a\le b$ are distinct. Let $S_n$ denote the maximum cardinality of a Sidon set in $\{1,2,\dots,n\}$. For any positive integer $\ell$, the sum $\sum_{a\in S} a^{\ell}$, taken over any Sidon set $S\subset\{1,2,\dots,n\}$ with $|S|=S_n$, has an asymptotic expansion as $n\to\infty$. What is the exact leading term of this asymptotic expansion, expressed as a function of $n$ and $\ell$?

Let $f:\mathbb{R}\to \mathbb{R}$ be a continuous function, let $\rho>0$, and let $\omega:[0,\rho]\to [0,+\infty]$ be a continuous increasing function such that $\lim_{\xi\to \rho^-}\int_0^\xi\omega(x)dx=+\infty$. Consider the space $C^0([0,1])\times C^0([0,1])$ endowed with the norm $\|(\alpha,\beta)\|=\int_0^1|\alpha(t)|dt+\int_0^1|\beta(t)|dt$. It is a theorem that the following two assertions are equivalent:

(a) the restriction of $f$ to the interval $[-c, c]$ is not constant;
(b) for every convex set $S\subseteq C^0([0,1])\times C^0([0,1])$ dense in $C^0([0,1])\times C^0([0,1])$, there exists $(\alpha,\beta)\in S$ such that the boundary value problem
$$ -\omega\left(\int_0^1|u'(t)|^2dt\right)u''=\beta(t)f(u)+\alpha(t) \quad \text{in } [0,1], $$
$$ u(0)=u(1)=0, $$
$$ \int_0^1|u'(t)|^2dt<\rho $$
has at least two classical solutions.

What is the exact value of $c$ in terms of $\rho$?

Let $\alpha$ be the normal number in base 2 constructed by Mordechay Levin. Let $D_N$ be the discrepancy of the sequence of fractional parts $(\{2^n \alpha\})_{n=0}^{N-1}$. It is known that $N \cdot D_N = \mathcal{O}((\log N)^p)$ for some integer $p$, and that this estimate is best possible in the sense that $N \cdot D_N \geq c \cdot (\log N)^p$ for infinitely many $N$, where $c > 0$ is a constant. What is the exact integer value of $p$?

Let $p>3$ be a prime number, and let $r$ be a positive integer. Consider the ring of invariants of the elementary abelian $p$-group $(\mathbb Z/p\mathbb Z)^r$ for a $3$-dimensional generic representation. It is known that this ring of invariants is a complete intersection ring. What is its embedding dimension as a function of $r$?

Consider the Hard-Core (HC) model with a countable set $\mathbb{Z}$ of spin values on a Cayley tree of order $k \geq 2$, defined by an activity function $\lambda_i > 0$ for $i \in \mathbb{Z}$, where admissible configurations satisfy $\sigma(x)\sigma(y) = 0$ for any nearest neighbors $x, y$. Let $\Lambda = \sum_i \lambda_i$. There exists a critical value $\Lambda_{\rm cr}(k)$ such that if $0 < \Lambda \leq \Lambda_{\rm cr}(k)$, there is exactly one two-periodic Gibbs measure, and if $\Lambda > \Lambda_{\rm cr}(k)$, there are exactly three two-periodic Gibbs measures. What is the value of $\Lambda_{\rm cr}(k)$ as a function of $k$?

In the study of Generalized Degrees of Freedom (GDoF) in $K$-user interference networks where all interference is treated as noise, what is the precise extremal GDoF gain of optimal power control over binary (on/off) power control for a network with $K=6$ users?

By applying a limiting procedure on kinematic invariants to the integral over the positive tropical Grassmannian for the biadjoint scalar partial amplitude $m_n$, one obtains an integral representation for $n$-point $\phi^4$ amplitudes. The support of the Dirac delta functions in this representation decomposes into $C_q$ regions, where $C_q$ is the $q$-th Catalan number. What is the index $q$ as a function of the number of external particles $n$?

Let $\Pi_\lambda$ denote the projection operator to the vector space spanned by the eigenfunctions of the Hermite operator $\mathcal{H}=-\Delta+|x|^2$ on $\mathbb{R}^d$ with eigenvalue $\lambda$. The optimal $L^2 \to L^p$ bounds on $\Pi_\lambda$ for $2 \le p \le \infty$ were established by the works of Karadzhov and Koch-Tataru, except for a single critical endpoint value of $p$ where the bound remained an open problem. For $d \ge 3$, what is this missing endpoint value of $p$ as a function of $d$?

In the study of uniform internally heated convection in the limit of infinite Prandtl number, consider a fluid in a horizontally-periodic layer between no-slip isothermal boundaries. Let $\overline{\langle wT \rangle}$ be the mean vertical convective heat transport, and let $R$ be the nondimensional flux Rayleigh number. By combining the background method with a minimum principle for the fluid's temperature and Hardy-Rellich inequalities to exploit the link between vertical velocity and temperature, a rigorous bound of the form $\overline{\langle wT \rangle} \leq \frac{1}{2} - c R^{-2}$ has been proven. What is the exact value of the constant $c$ established in this bound?

Let $A \subset \mathbb{R}^d$ be a finite set of $n$ points in general position. Let $\Delta(A)$ denote the spread of $A$, defined as the ratio of the maximum pairwise distance to the minimum pairwise distance among the points in $A$. For a fixed constant $\alpha > 0$, let $\gamma_d(n)$ denote the largest integer such that any such set $A$ satisfying $\Delta(A) \leq \alpha n^{1/d}$ is guaranteed to contain a subset of at least $\gamma_d(n)$ points in convex position. It is known that $\gamma_2(n) = \Theta(n^{1/3})$. There exists a constant $c$ such that $\gamma_3(n) = \Theta(n^c)$. What is the exact value of $c$?

Let $R$ be a real closed field. Up to interdefinability (i.e., identifying structures that yield the same collection of definable sets over $R$), what is the exact number of proper reducts of the field $R$ that expand the underlying unordered $R$-vector space structure?

Let $P$ be a finite, graded poset of rank $n$. Consider the action of promotion on the set of $P$-strict labelings of $P$ with labels at most $q$. This dynamical system is in equivariant bijection with the set of $Q$-partitions under rowmotion, where the poset $Q$ is the product of $P$ and a chain of $k$ elements. What is the value of $k$ as a function of $q$ and $n$?

Consider the scaling limit of random quadrangulations decorated by a self-avoiding polygon. This limit converges to a Liouville quantum gravity (LQG) sphere decorated by an $\text{SLE}_{\kappa}$ loop. What is the exact value of the parameter $\kappa$?

For any $\frac{1}{2}\leq \beta \leq 1$, there exists a group $G$ from the family of lamplighter diagonal products and a random walk $W_n$ on $G$ such that the expected distance from the origin satisfies $\mathbb{E}|W_n| \simeq n^\beta$, and the sequence of distances $|W_n|$ satisfies the Law of Iterated Logarithm bounds:
$$0<\limsup_{n\to\infty} \frac{|W_n|}{n^\beta(\log\log n)^\gamma}<\infty$$
and
$$0<\liminf_{n\to\infty} \frac{|W_n|(\log\log n)^\gamma}{n^\beta}<\infty.$$
What is the exact value of the exponent $\gamma$ as a function of $\beta$?

Let $X$ be an $n$-dimensional smooth well-formed weighted complete intersection that is not isomorphic to the usual projective space $\mathbb{P}^n$. If $X$ is an $l$-Fano manifold, what is the exact upper bound for $l$ as a function of $n$?

Let $G$ be a graph, let $m$ be a positive integer, and let $g, f,$ and $f'$ be integer-valued functions on $V(G)$ such that $0 \le g \le f$, $f \ge m$, and $f' \ge m$. Suppose that $G$ contains a $(g,f)$-factor and an $m$-tree-connected $(m,f')$-factor. A theorem generalizing a 2003 result of Tokuda, Xu, and Wang (who proved the $m=1$ case) guarantees that $G$ also contains an $m$-tree-connected $(g, h)$-factor. What is the function $h$ expressed in terms of $f, f'$, and $m$?

Let $U$ be a random unitary matrix drawn from the Hua-Pickrell distribution $\mu_{\mathrm{U}(n+m)}^{(\delta)}$ on the unitary group $\mathrm{U}(n+m)$, where $m$ is a fixed positive integer and $\delta\in\mathbb{C}$ satisfies $\mathrm{Re}\,\delta>-1/2$. The eigenvalues of the $n \times n$ truncated unitary matrix $[U_{i,j}]_{1\leq i,j\leq n}$ form a determinantal point process $\mathscr{X}_n^{(m,\delta)}$ on the unit disc $\mathbb{D}$. As $n\to\infty$, $\mathscr{X}_n^{(m,\delta)}$ converges to a limiting determinantal point process on $\mathbb{D}$ with respect to the reference measure $d\mu(z)=\frac{m}{\pi}(1-|z|)^{m-1}d\sigma(z)$, where $d\sigma(z)$ is the Lebesgue measure on $\mathbb{D}$. What is the weighted Bergman kernel $K(z,w)$ of this limiting determinantal point process?

Consider the fixed-template constraint satisfaction problem (CSP) whose template is given by an oriented tree. What is the minimum number of vertices a tree can have such that there exists an orientation of the tree for which the corresponding CSP is NP-complete?
What is the number of Dyck paths of semilength $n$ having exactly $i$ returns to ground level and exactly $j$ peaks?

Consider the two-dimensional Keller-Segel model with logarithmic interaction and a nonlocal reaction term given by $u\left(M_0 - \int_{\mathbb{R}^2} u \,dx\right)$, where $u$ is the population density, $M_0$ is the growth parameter, and $m_0$ is the initial mass. There exists a critical value $M_c$ that dictates the global existence and blow-up of solutions: if $m_0 < M_c$ and $M_0 < M_c$, general solutions exist globally in time, whereas infinite time blow-up occurs for the critical case $M_0 = M_c$. What is the exact value of $M_c$?

Consider a sequence of one-dimensional power-law small-world graphs on $n$ nodes, obtained as the union of an $n$-node cycle with additional long-range random edges, where each pair of nodes at distance $L$ on the cycle is connected by a long-range edge with probability proportional to $1/L^\alpha$. Suppose full-bond percolation is performed on this graph by retaining each edge independently with probability $p$. There exists a critical threshold $\alpha_c$ such that for any $\alpha > \alpha_c$ and for every fixed $p < 1$, all connected components of the resulting percolation subgraph have size $O(\log n)$ with high probability as $n \to \infty$. Furthermore, for $\alpha < \alpha_c$, there exists some fixed $p < 1$ such that with high probability the percolation subgraph contains a connected component of size $\Omega(n)$. What is the exact numerical value of $\alpha_c$?

Let $G$ be a triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices, and let $\mu_1(G)$ and $\mu_n(G)$ denote its largest and smallest adjacency eigenvalues, respectively. Brandt proved that for regular triangle-free graphs, the inequality $\mu_1(G) + \mu_n(G) \leq c n$ holds for a specific constant $c$. It was later shown that the condition on regularity is not necessary and the same inequality holds for all triangle-free graphs. What is the value of the constant $c$?

Consider the family of bilinear Hilbert transforms defined for suitable functions $f_1, f_2$ by
$$ \mathrm{BHT}_\beta [f_1, f_2] (x) := \mathrm{p.v.} \int_{\mathbb{R}} f_1 (x - t) f_2 (x + \beta t) \frac{\mathrm{d} t}{t}. $$
